speaker: ELSINORE
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] ELSINORE. A platform before the Castle.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. Who's there?
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: FRANCISCO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] FRANCISCO. Nay, answer me. Stand and unfold yourself.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. Long live the King!
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: FRANCISCO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] FRANCISCO. You come most carefully upon your hour.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. 'Tis now struck twelve. Get thee to bed, Francisco.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: FRANCISCO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] FRANCISCO. For this relief much thanks. 'Tis bitter cold, And I am sick at heart.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. Have you had quiet guard?
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: FRANCISCO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] FRANCISCO. Not a mouse stirring.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. Well, good night. If you do meet Horatio and Marcellus, The rivals of my watch, bid them make haste.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: FRANCISCO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] FRANCISCO. I think I hear them. Stand, ho! Who is there?
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] HORATIO. Friends to this ground.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] MARCELLUS. And liegemen to the Dane.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: FRANCISCO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] FRANCISCO. Give you good night.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] MARCELLUS. O, farewell, honest soldier. Who hath reliev'd you?
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: FRANCISCO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] FRANCISCO. Bernardo hath my place. Give you good night. Exit.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] MARCELLUS. Holla, Bernardo!
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. Say- What, is Horatio there ?
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] HORATIO. A piece of him.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. Welcome, Horatio. Welcome, good Marcellus.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] MARCELLUS. What, has this thing appear'd again to-night?
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. I have seen nothing.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] MARCELLUS. Horatio says 'tis but our fantasy, And will not let belief take hold of him Touching this dreaded sight, twice seen of us. Therefore I have entreated him along, With us to watch the minutes of this night, That, if again this apparition come, He may approve our eyes and speak to it.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] HORATIO. Tush, tush, 'twill not appear.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. Sit down awhile, And let us once again assail your ears, That are so fortified against our story, What we two nights have seen.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] HORATIO. Well, sit we down, And let us hear Bernardo speak of this.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. Last night of all, When yond same star that's westward from the pole Had made his course t' illume that part of heaven Where now it burns, Marcellus and myself, The bell then beating one-
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] MARCELLUS. Peace! break thee off! Look where it comes again!
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. In the same figure, like the King that's dead.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] MARCELLUS. Thou art a scholar; speak to it, Horatio.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. Looks it not like the King? Mark it, Horatio.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] HORATIO. Most like. It harrows me with fear and wonder.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. It would be spoke to.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] MARCELLUS. Question it, Horatio.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] HORATIO. What art thou that usurp'st this time of night Together with that fair and warlike form In which the majesty of buried Denmark Did sometimes march? By heaven I charge thee speak!
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] MARCELLUS. It is offended.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. See, it stalks away!
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] HORATIO. Stay! Speak, speak! I charge thee speak!
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] MARCELLUS. 'Tis gone and will not answer.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. How now, Horatio? You tremble and look pale. Is not this something more than fantasy? What think you on't?
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] HORATIO. Before my God, I might not this believe Without the sensible and true avouch Of mine own eyes.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] MARCELLUS. Is it not like the King?
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] HORATIO. As thou art to thyself. Such was the very armour he had on When he th' ambitious Norway combated. So frown'd he once when, in an angry parle, He smote the sledded Polacks on the ice. 'Tis strange.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] MARCELLUS. Thus twice before, and jump at this dead hour, With martial stalk hath he gone by our watch.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] HORATIO. In what particular thought to work I know not; But, in the gross and scope of my opinion, This bodes some strange eruption to our state.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] MARCELLUS. Good now, sit down, and tell me he that knows, Why this same strict and most observant watch So nightly toils the subject of the land, And why such daily cast of brazen cannon And foreign mart for implements of war; Why such impress of shipwrights, whose sore task Does not divide the Sunday from the week. What might be toward, that this sweaty haste Doth make the night joint-labourer with the day? Who is't that can inform me?
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] HORATIO. That can I. At least, the whisper goes so. Our last king, Whose image even but now appear'd to us, Was, as you know, by Fortinbras of Norway, Thereto prick'd on by a most emulate pride, Dar'd to the combat; in which our valiant Hamlet (For so this side of our known world esteem'd him) Did slay this Fortinbras; who, by a seal'd compact, Well ratified by law and heraldry, Did forfeit, with his life, all those his lands Which he stood seiz'd of, to the conqueror; Against the which a moiety competent Was gaged by our king; which had return'd To the inheritance of Fortinbras, Had he been vanquisher, as, by the same cov'nant And carriage of the article design'd, His fell to Hamlet. Now, sir, young Fortinbras, Of unimproved mettle hot and full, Hath in the skirts of Norway, here and there, Shark'd up a list of lawless resolutes, For food and diet, to some enterprise That hath a stomach in't; which is no other, As it doth well appear unto our state, But to recover of us, by strong hand And terms compulsatory, those foresaid lands So by his father lost; and this, I take it, Is the main motive of our preparations, The source of this our watch, and the chief head Of this post-haste and romage in the land.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. I think it be no other but e'en so. Well may it sort that this portentous figure Comes armed through our watch, so like the King That was and is the question of these wars.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] HORATIO. A mote it is to trouble the mind's eye. In the most high and palmy state of Rome, A little ere the mightiest Julius fell, The graves stood tenantless, and the sheeted dead Did squeak and gibber in the Roman streets; As stars with trains of fire, and dews of blood, Disasters in the sun; and the moist star Upon whose influence Neptune's empire stands Was sick almost to doomsday with eclipse. And even the like precurse of fierce events, As harbingers preceding still the fates And prologue to the omen coming on, Have heaven and earth together demonstrated Unto our climature and countrymen.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] MARCELLUS. Shall I strike at it with my partisan?
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] HORATIO. Do, if it will not stand.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. 'Tis here!
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] HORATIO. 'Tis here!
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] MARCELLUS. 'Tis gone!
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: BERNARDO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] BERNARDO. It was about to speak, when the cock crew.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] HORATIO. And then it started, like a guilty thing Upon a fearful summons. I have heard The cock, that is the trumpet to the morn, Doth with his lofty and shrill-sounding throat Awake the god of day; and at his warning, Whether in sea or fire, in earth or air, Th' extravagant and erring spirit hies To his confine; and of the truth herein This present object made probation.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] MARCELLUS. It faded on the crowing of the cock. Some say that ever, 'gainst that season comes Wherein our Saviour's birth is celebrated, The bird of dawning singeth all night long; And then, they say, no spirit dare stir abroad, The nights are wholesome, then no planets strike, No fairy takes, nor witch hath power to charm, So hallow'd and so gracious is the time.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.] [Keywords: ghost, Horatio] HORATIO. So have I heard and do in part believe it. But look, the morn, in russet mantle clad, Walks o'er the dew of yon high eastward hill. Break we our watch up; and by my advice Let us impart what we have seen to-night Unto young Hamlet; for, upon my life, This spirit, dumb to us, will speak to him. Do you consent we shall acquaint him with it, As needful in our loves, fitting our duty? Let's do't, I pray; and I this morning know Where we shall find him most conveniently. Exeunt.
scene_summary: Guards and Horatio see the ghost and decide to tell Hamlet.

speaker: ELSINORE
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] ELSINORE. A room of state in the Castle.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: FLOURISH
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] FLOURISH. Lords Attendant.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] CLAUDIUS. Though yet of Hamlet our dear brother's death The memory be green, and that it us befitted To bear our hearts in grief, and our whole kingdom To be contracted in one brow of woe, Yet so far hath discretion fought with nature That we with wisest sorrow think on him Together with remembrance of ourselves. Therefore our sometime sister, now our queen, Th' imperial jointress to this warlike state, Have we, as 'twere with a defeated joy, With an auspicious, and a dropping eye, With mirth in funeral, and with dirge in marriage, In equal scale weighing delight and dole, Taken to wife; nor have we herein barr'd Your better wisdoms, which have freely gone With this affair along. For all, our thanks. Now follows, that you know, young Fortinbras, Holding a weak supposal of our worth, Or thinking by our late dear brother's death Our state to be disjoint and out of frame, Colleagued with this dream of his advantage, He hath not fail'd to pester us with message Importing the surrender of those lands Lost by his father, with all bands of law, To our most valiant brother. So much for him. Now for ourself and for this time of meeting. Thus much the business is: we have here writ To Norway, uncle of young Fortinbras, Who, impotent and bedrid, scarcely hears Of this his nephew's purpose, to suppress His further gait herein, in that the levies, The lists, and full proportions are all made Out of his subject; and we here dispatch You, good Cornelius, and you, Voltemand, For bearers of this greeting to old Norway, Giving to you no further personal power To business with the King, more than the scope Of these dilated articles allow. Farewell, and let your haste commend your duty.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: COR
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] COR. , Volt. In that, and all things, will we show our duty.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] CLAUDIUS. We doubt it nothing. Heartily farewell.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: LAERTES
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] LAERTES. My dread lord, Your leave and favour to return to France; From whence though willingly I came to Denmark To show my duty in your coronation, Yet now I must confess, that duty done, My thoughts and wishes bend again toward France And bow them to your gracious leave and pardon.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] CLAUDIUS. Have you your father's leave? What says Polonius?
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] POLONIUS. He hath, my lord, wrung from me my slow leave By laboursome petition, and at last Upon his will I seal'd my hard consent. I do beseech you give him leave to go.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] CLAUDIUS. Take thy fair hour, Laertes. Time be thine, And thy best graces spend it at thy will! But now, my cousin Hamlet, and my son-
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. A little more than kin, and less than kind!
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] CLAUDIUS. How is it that the clouds still hang on you?
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. Not so, my lord. I am too much i' th' sun.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] GERTRUDE. Good Hamlet, cast thy nighted colour off, And let thine eye look like a friend on Denmark. Do not for ever with thy vailed lids Seek for thy noble father in the dust. Thou know'st 'tis common. All that lives must die, Passing through nature to eternity.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. Ay, madam, it is common.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] GERTRUDE. If it be, Why seems it so particular with thee?
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. Seems, madam, Nay, it is. I know not 'seems.' 'Tis not alone my inky cloak, good mother, Nor customary suits of solemn black, Nor windy suspiration of forc'd breath, No, nor the fruitful river in the eye, Nor the dejected havior of the visage, Together with all forms, moods, shapes of grief, 'That can denote me truly. These indeed seem, For they are actions that a man might play; But I have that within which passeth show- These but the trappings and the suits of woe.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] CLAUDIUS. 'Tis sweet and commendable in your nature, Hamlet, To give these mourning duties to your father; But you must know, your father lost a father; That father lost, lost his, and the survivor bound In filial obligation for some term To do obsequious sorrow. But to persever In obstinate condolement is a course Of impious stubbornness. 'Tis unmanly grief; It shows a will most incorrect to heaven, A heart unfortified, a mind impatient, An understanding simple and unschool'd; For what we know must be, and is as common As any the most vulgar thing to sense, Why should we in our peevish opposition Take it to heart? Fie! 'tis a fault to heaven, A fault against the dead, a fault to nature, To reason most absurd, whose common theme Is death of fathers, and who still hath cried, From the first corse till he that died to-day, 'This must be so.' We pray you throw to earth This unprevailing woe, and think of us As of a father; for let the world take note You are the most immediate to our throne, And with no less nobility of love Than that which dearest father bears his son Do I impart toward you. For your intent In going back to school in Wittenberg, It is most retrograde to our desire; And we beseech you, bend you to remain Here in the cheer and comfort of our eye, Our chiefest courtier, cousin, and our son.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] GERTRUDE. Let not thy mother lose her prayers, Hamlet. I pray thee stay with us, go not to Wittenberg.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. I shall in all my best obey you, madam.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] CLAUDIUS. Why, 'tis a loving and a fair reply. Be as ourself in Denmark. Madam, come. This gentle and unforc'd accord of Hamlet Sits smiling to my heart; in grace whereof, No jocund health that Denmark drinks to-day But the great cannon to the clouds shall tell, And the King's rouse the heaven shall bruit again, Respeaking earthly thunder. Come away.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: FLOURISH
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] FLOURISH. Exeunt all but Hamlet.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. O that this too too solid flesh would melt, Thaw, and resolve itself into a dew! Or that the Everlasting had not fix'd His canon 'gainst self-slaughter! O God! God! How weary, stale, flat, and unprofitable Seem to me all the uses of this world! Fie on't! ah, fie! 'Tis an unweeded garden That grows to seed; things rank and gross in nature Possess it merely. That it should come to this! But two months dead! Nay, not so much, not two. So excellent a king, that was to this Hyperion to a satyr; so loving to my mother That he might not beteem the winds of heaven Visit her face too roughly. Heaven and earth! Must I remember? Why, she would hang on him As if increase of appetite had grown By what it fed on; and yet, within a month- Let me not think on't! Frailty, thy name is woman!- A little month, or ere those shoes were old With which she followed my poor father's body Like Niobe, all tears- why she, even she (O God! a beast that wants discourse of reason Would have mourn'd longer) married with my uncle; My father's brother, but no more like my father Than I to Hercules. Within a month, Ere yet the salt of most unrighteous tears Had left the flushing in her galled eyes, She married. O, most wicked speed, to post With such dexterity to incestuous sheets! It is not, nor it cannot come to good. But break my heart, for I must hold my tongue!
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HORATIO. Hail to your lordship!
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. I am glad to see you well. Horatio!- or I do forget myself.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HORATIO. The same, my lord, and your poor servant ever.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. Sir, my good friend- I'll change that name with you. And what make you from Wittenberg, Horatio? Marcellus?

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] MARCELLUS. My good lord!

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. I am very glad to see you.- Good even, sir.- But what, in faith, make you from Wittenberg?

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HORATIO. A truant disposition, good my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. I would not hear your enemy say so, Nor shall you do my ear that violence To make it truster of your own report Against yourself. I know you are no truant. But what is your affair in Elsinore? We'll teach you to drink deep ere you depart.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HORATIO. My lord, I came to see your father's funeral.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. I prithee do not mock me, fellow student. I think it was to see my mother's wedding.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HORATIO. Indeed, my lord, it followed hard upon.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. Thrift, thrift, Horatio! The funeral bak'd meats Did coldly furnish forth the marriage tables. Would I had met my dearest foe in heaven Or ever I had seen that day, Horatio! My father- methinks I see my father.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HORATIO. O, where, my lord?

text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. In my mind's eye, Horatio.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. The King my father?

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HORATIO. Season your admiration for a while With an attent ear, till I may deliver Upon the witness of these gentlemen, This marvel to you.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. For God's love let me hear!
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HORATIO. Two nights together had these gentlemen (Marcellus and Bernardo) on their watch In the dead vast and middle of the night Been thus encount'red. A figure like your father, Armed at point exactly, cap-a-pe, Appears before them and with solemn march Goes slow and stately by them. Thrice he walk'd By their oppress'd and fear-surprised eyes, Within his truncheon's length; whilst they distill'd Almost to jelly with the act of fear, Stand dumb and speak not to him. This to me In dreadful secrecy impart they did, And I with them the third night kept the watch; Where, as they had deliver'd, both in time, Form of the thing, each word made true and good, The apparition comes. I knew your father. These hands are not more like.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. But where was this?

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] MARCELLUS. My lord, upon the platform where we watch'd.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. Did you not speak to it?

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HORATIO. My lord, I did; But answer made it none. Yet once methought It lifted up it head and did address Itself to motion, like as it would speak; But even then the morning cock crew loud, And at the sound it shrunk in haste away And vanish'd from our sight.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. 'Tis very strange.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HORATIO. As I do live, my honour'd lord, 'tis true; And we did think it writ down in our duty To let you know of it.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. Indeed, indeed, sirs. But this troubles me. Hold you the watch to-night? Both We do, my lord.

text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. Arm'd, say you?

text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. And fix'd his eyes upon you?

text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. Very like, very like. Stay'd it long?

text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HORATIO. It was, as I have seen it in his life, A sable silver'd.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. I will watch to-night. Perchance 'twill walk again.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HORATIO. I warr'nt it will.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. If it assume my noble father's person, I'll speak to it, though hell itself should gape And bid me hold my peace. I pray you all, If you have hitherto conceal'd this sight, Let it be tenable in your silence still; And whatsoever else shall hap to-night, Give it an understanding but no tongue. I will requite your loves. So, fare you well. Upon the platform, 'twixt eleven and twelve, I'll visit you.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: ALL
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] ALL. Our duty to your honour.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.] [Keywords: Claudius, Hamlet, mourning] HAMLET. Your loves, as mine to you. Farewell.
scene_summary: Claudius addresses the court; Hamlet mourns and resents Gertrude’s remarriage.

speaker: ELSINORE
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] ELSINORE. A room in the house of Polonius.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: LAERTES
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] LAERTES. My necessaries are embark'd. Farewell. And, sister, as the winds give benefit And convoy is assistant, do not sleep, But let me hear from you.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] OPHELIA. Do you doubt that?
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: LAERTES
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] LAERTES. For Hamlet, and the trifling of his favour, Hold it a fashion, and a toy in blood; A violet in the youth of primy nature, Forward, not permanent- sweet, not lasting; The perfume and suppliance of a minute; No more.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] OPHELIA. No more but so?
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: LAERTES
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] LAERTES. Think it no more. For nature crescent does not grow alone In thews and bulk; but as this temple waxes, The inward service of the mind and soul Grows wide withal. Perhaps he loves you now, And now no soil nor cautel doth besmirch The virtue of his will; but you must fear, His greatness weigh'd, his will is not his own; For he himself is subject to his birth. He may not, as unvalued persons do, Carve for himself, for on his choice depends The safety and health of this whole state, And therefore must his choice be circumscrib'd Unto the voice and yielding of that body Whereof he is the head. Then if he says he loves you, It fits your wisdom so far to believe it As he in his particular act and place May give his saying deed; which is no further Than the main voice of Denmark goes withal. Then weigh what loss your honour may sustain If with too credent ear you list his songs, Or lose your heart, or your chaste treasure open To his unmast'red importunity. Fear it, Ophelia, fear it, my dear sister, And keep you in the rear of your affection, Out of the shot and danger of desire. The chariest maid is prodigal enough If she unmask her beauty to the moon. Virtue itself scopes not calumnious strokes. The canker galls the infants of the spring Too oft before their buttons be disclos'd, And in the morn and liquid dew of youth Contagious blastments are most imminent. Be wary then; best safety lies in fear. Youth to itself rebels, though none else near.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] OPHELIA. I shall th' effect of this good lesson keep As watchman to my heart. But, good my brother, Do not as some ungracious pastors do, Show me the steep and thorny way to heaven, Whiles, like a puff'd and reckless libertine, Himself the primrose path of dalliance treads And recks not his own rede.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: LAERTES
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] LAERTES. O, fear me not!
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] POLONIUS. Yet here, Laertes? Aboard, aboard, for shame! The wind sits in the shoulder of your sail, And you are stay'd for. There- my blessing with thee! And these few precepts in thy memory Look thou character. Give thy thoughts no tongue, Nor any unproportion'd thought his act. Be thou familiar, but by no means vulgar: Those friends thou hast, and their adoption tried, Grapple them unto thy soul with hoops of steel; But do not dull thy palm with entertainment Of each new-hatch'd, unfledg'd comrade. Beware Of entrance to a quarrel; but being in, Bear't that th' opposed may beware of thee. Give every man thine ear, but few thy voice; Take each man's censure, but reserve thy judgment. Costly thy habit as thy purse can buy, But not express'd in fancy; rich, not gaudy; For the apparel oft proclaims the man, And they in France of the best rank and station Are most select and generous, chief in that. Neither a borrower nor a lender be; For loan oft loses both itself and friend, And borrowing dulls the edge of husbandry. This above all- to thine own self be true, And it must follow, as the night the day, Thou canst not then be false to any man.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: FAREWELL
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] FAREWELL. My blessing season this in thee!
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: LAERTES
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] LAERTES. Most humbly do I take my leave, my lord.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] POLONIUS. The time invites you. Go, your servants tend.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: LAERTES
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] LAERTES. Farewell, Ophelia, and remember well What I have said to you.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] OPHELIA. 'Tis in my memory lock'd, And you yourself shall keep the key of it.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: LAERTES
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] LAERTES. Farewell. Exit.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] POLONIUS. What is't, Ophelia, he hath said to you?
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] OPHELIA. So please you, something touching the Lord Hamlet.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] POLONIUS. Marry, well bethought! 'Tis told me he hath very oft of late Given private time to you, and you yourself Have of your audience been most free and bounteous. If it be so- as so 'tis put on me, And that in way of caution- I must tell you You do not understand yourself so clearly As it behooves my daughter and your honour. What is between you? Give me up the truth.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] OPHELIA. He hath, my lord, of late made many tenders Of his affection to me.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] POLONIUS. Affection? Pooh! You speak like a green girl, Unsifted in such perilous circumstance. Do you believe his tenders, as you call them?
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] OPHELIA. I do not know, my lord, what I should think,
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] POLONIUS. Marry, I will teach you! Think yourself a baby That you have ta'en these tenders for true pay, Which are not sterling. Tender yourself more dearly, Or (not to crack the wind of the poor phrase, Running it thus) you'll tender me a fool.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] OPHELIA. My lord, he hath importun'd me with love In honourable fashion.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] POLONIUS. Ay, fashion you may call it. Go to, go to!
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] OPHELIA. And hath given countenance to his speech, my lord, With almost all the holy vows of heaven.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] POLONIUS. Ay, springes to catch woodcocks! I do know, When the blood burns, how prodigal the soul Lends the tongue vows. These blazes, daughter, Giving more light than heat, extinct in both Even in their promise, as it is a-making, You must not take for fire. From this time Be something scanter of your maiden presence. Set your entreatments at a higher rate Than a command to parley. For Lord Hamlet, Believe so much in him, that he is young, And with a larger tether may he walk Than may be given you. In few, Ophelia, Do not believe his vows; for they are brokers, Not of that dye which their investments show, But mere implorators of unholy suits, Breathing like sanctified and pious bawds, The better to beguile. This is for all: I would not, in plain terms, from this time forth Have you so slander any moment leisure As to give words or talk with the Lord Hamlet. Look to't, I charge you. Come your ways.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.] [Keywords: Ophelia, Polonius] OPHELIA. I shall obey, my lord.
scene_summary: Laertes and Polonius warn Ophelia about Hamlet.

speaker: ELSINORE
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] ELSINORE. The platform before the Castle.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HAMLET. The air bites shrewdly; it is very cold.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HORATIO. It is a nipping and an eager air.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HAMLET. What hour now?
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HORATIO. I think it lacks of twelve.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] MARCELLUS. No, it is struck.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HORATIO. Indeed? I heard it not. It then draws near the season Wherein the spirit held his wont to walk. A flourish of trumpets, and two pieces go off. What does this mean, my lord?
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HAMLET. The King doth wake to-night and takes his rouse, Keeps wassail, and the swagg'ring upspring reels, And, as he drains his draughts of Rhenish down, The kettledrum and trumpet thus bray out The triumph of his pledge.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HORATIO. Is it a custom?
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HAMLET. Ay, marry, is't; But to my mind, though I am native here And to the manner born, it is a custom More honour'd in the breach than the observance. This heavy-headed revel east and west Makes us traduc'd and tax'd of other nations; They clip us drunkards and with swinish phrase Soil our addition; and indeed it takes From our achievements, though perform'd at height, The pith and marrow of our attribute. So oft it chances in particular men That, for some vicious mole of nature in them, As in their birth,- wherein they are not guilty, Since nature cannot choose his origin,- By the o'ergrowth of some complexion, Oft breaking down the pales and forts of reason, Or by some habit that too much o'erleavens The form of plausive manners, that these men Carrying, I say, the stamp of one defect, Being nature's livery, or fortune's star, Their virtues else- be they as pure as grace, As infinite as man may undergo- Shall in the general censure take corruption From that particular fault. The dram of e'il Doth all the noble substance often dout To his own scandal.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HORATIO. Look, my lord, it comes!
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HAMLET. Angels and ministers of grace defend us! Be thou a spirit of health or goblin damn'd, Bring with thee airs from heaven or blasts from hell, Be thy intents wicked or charitable, Thou com'st in such a questionable shape That I will speak to thee. I'll call thee Hamlet, King, father, royal Dane. O, answer me? Let me not burst in ignorance, but tell Why thy canoniz'd bones, hearsed in death, Have burst their cerements; why the sepulchre Wherein we saw thee quietly inurn'd, Hath op'd his ponderous and marble jaws To cast thee up again. What may this mean That thou, dead corse, again in complete steel, Revisits thus the glimpses of the moon, Making night hideous, and we fools of nature So horridly to shake our disposition With thoughts beyond the reaches of our souls? Say, why is this? wherefore? What should we do? Ghost beckons Hamlet.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HORATIO. It beckons you to go away with it, As if it some impartment did desire To you alone.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] MARCELLUS. Look with what courteous action It waves you to a more removed ground. But do not go with it!
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HORATIO. No, by no means!
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HAMLET. It will not speak. Then will I follow it.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HORATIO. Do not, my lord!
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HAMLET. Why, what should be the fear? I do not set my life at a pin's fee; And for my soul, what can it do to that, Being a thing immortal as itself? It waves me forth again. I'll follow it.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HORATIO. What if it tempt you toward the flood, my lord, Or to the dreadful summit of the cliff That beetles o'er his base into the sea, And there assume some other, horrible form Which might deprive your sovereignty of reason And draw you into madness? Think of it. The very place puts toys of desperation, Without more motive, into every brain That looks so many fadoms to the sea And hears it roar beneath.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HAMLET. It waves me still. Go on. I'll follow thee.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] MARCELLUS. You shall not go, my lord.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HAMLET. Hold off your hands!
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HORATIO. Be rul'd. You shall not go.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HAMLET. My fate cries out And makes each petty artire in this body As hardy as the Nemean lion's nerve.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HORATIO. He waxes desperate with imagination.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] MARCELLUS. Let's follow. 'Tis not fit thus to obey him.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HORATIO. Have after. To what issue will this come?
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] MARCELLUS. Something is rotten in the state of Denmark.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] HORATIO. Heaven will direct it.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.] [Keywords: ghost, Hamlet] MARCELLUS. Nay, let's follow him.
scene_summary: Hamlet waits for the ghost and follows it.

speaker: ELSINORE
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] ELSINORE. The Castle. Another part of the fortifications.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. Whither wilt thou lead me? Speak! I'll go no further.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: GHOST
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] GHOST. My hour is almost come, When I to sulph'rous and tormenting flames Must render up myself.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. Alas, poor ghost!
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: GHOST
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] GHOST. Pity me not, but lend thy serious hearing To what I shall unfold.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. Speak. I am bound to hear.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: GHOST
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] GHOST. So art thou to revenge, when thou shalt hear.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: GHOST
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] GHOST. I am thy father's spirit, Doom'd for a certain term to walk the night, And for the day confin'd to fast in fires, Till the foul crimes done in my days of nature Are burnt and purg'd away. But that I am forbid To tell the secrets of my prison house, I could a tale unfold whose lightest word Would harrow up thy soul, freeze thy young blood, Make thy two eyes, like stars, start from their spheres, Thy knotted and combined locks to part, And each particular hair to stand on end Like quills upon the fretful porcupine. But this eternal blazon must not be To ears of flesh and blood. List, list, O, list! If thou didst ever thy dear father love-
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: GHOST
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] GHOST. Revenge his foul and most unnatural murther.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: GHOST
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] GHOST. Murther most foul, as in the best it is; But this most foul, strange, and unnatural.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. Haste me to know't, that I, with wings as swift As meditation or the thoughts of love, May sweep to my revenge.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: GHOST
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] GHOST. I find thee apt; And duller shouldst thou be than the fat weed That rots itself in ease on Lethe wharf, Wouldst thou not stir in this. Now, Hamlet, hear. 'Tis given out that, sleeping in my orchard, A serpent stung me. So the whole ear of Denmark Is by a forged process of my death Rankly abus'd. But know, thou noble youth, The serpent that did sting thy father's life Now wears his crown.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. O my prophetic soul! My uncle?
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: GHOST
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] GHOST. Ay, that incestuous, that adulterate beast, With witchcraft of his wit, with traitorous gifts- O wicked wit and gifts, that have the power So to seduce!- won to his shameful lust The will of my most seeming-virtuous queen. O Hamlet, what a falling-off was there, From me, whose love was of that dignity That it went hand in hand even with the vow I made to her in marriage, and to decline Upon a wretch whose natural gifts were poor To those of mine! But virtue, as it never will be mov'd, Though lewdness court it in a shape of heaven, So lust, though to a radiant angel link'd, Will sate itself in a celestial bed And prey on garbage. But soft! methinks I scent the morning air. Brief let me be. Sleeping within my orchard, My custom always of the afternoon, Upon my secure hour thy uncle stole, With juice of cursed hebona in a vial, And in the porches of my ears did pour The leperous distilment; whose effect Holds such an enmity with blood of man That swift as quicksilver it courses through The natural gates and alleys of the body, And with a sudden vigour it doth posset And curd, like eager droppings into milk, The thin and wholesome blood. So did it mine; And a most instant tetter bark'd about, Most lazar-like, with vile and loathsome crust All my smooth body. Thus was I, sleeping, by a brother's hand Of life, of crown, of queen, at once dispatch'd; Cut off even in the blossoms of my sin, Unhous'led, disappointed, unanel'd, No reckoning made, but sent to my account With all my imperfections on my head.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. O, horrible! O, horrible! most horrible!
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: GHOST
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] GHOST. If thou hast nature in thee, bear it not. Let not the royal bed of Denmark be A couch for luxury and damned incest. But, howsoever thou pursuest this act, Taint not thy mind, nor let thy soul contrive Against thy mother aught. Leave her to heaven, And to those thorns that in her bosom lodge To prick and sting her. Fare thee well at once. The glowworm shows the matin to be near And gins to pale his uneffectual fire. Adieu, adieu, adieu! Remember me. Exit.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. O all you host of heaven! O earth! What else? And shall I couple hell? Hold, hold, my heart! And you, my sinews, grow not instant old, But bear me stiffly up. Remember thee? Ay, thou poor ghost, while memory holds a seat In this distracted globe. Remember thee? Yea, from the table of my memory I'll wipe away all trivial fond records, All saws of books, all forms, all pressures past That youth and observation copied there, And thy commandment all alone shall live Within the book and volume of my brain, Unmix'd with baser matter. Yes, by heaven! O most pernicious woman! O villain, villain, smiling, damned villain! My tables! Meet it is I set it down That one may smile, and smile, and be a villain; At least I am sure it may be so in Denmark. So, uncle, there you are. Now to my word: It is 'Adieu, adieu! Remember me.' I have sworn't.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HORATIO. (within) My lord, my lord!
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] MARCELLUS. Lord Hamlet!
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HORATIO. Heaven secure him!
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] MARCELLUS. Illo, ho, ho, my lord!
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. Hillo, ho, ho, boy! Come, bird, come.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] MARCELLUS. How is't, my noble lord?
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HORATIO. What news, my lord?
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] MARCELLUS. O, wonderful!
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HORATIO. Good my lord, tell it.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. No, you will reveal it.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HORATIO. Not I, my lord, by heaven!
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] MARCELLUS. Nor I, my lord.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. How say you then? Would heart of man once think it? But you'll be secret?
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: BOTH
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] BOTH. Ay, by heaven, my lord.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. There's neer a villain dwelling in all Denmark But he's an arrant knave.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HORATIO. There needs no ghost, my lord, come from the grave To tell us this.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. Why, right! You are in the right! And so, without more circumstance at all, I hold it fit that we shake hands and part; You, as your business and desires shall point you, For every man hath business and desire, Such as it is; and for my own poor part, Look you, I'll go pray.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HORATIO. These are but wild and whirling words, my lord.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. I am sorry they offend you, heartily; Yes, faith, heartily.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HORATIO. There's no offence, my lord.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. Yes, by Saint Patrick, but there is, Horatio, And much offence too. Touching this vision here, It is an honest ghost, that let me tell you. For your desire to know what is between us, O'ermaster't as you may. And now, good friends, As you are friends, scholars, and soldiers, Give me one poor request.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HORATIO. What is't, my lord? We will.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. Never make known what you have seen to-night.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: BOTH
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] BOTH. My lord, we will not.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. Nay, but swear't.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HORATIO. In faith, My lord, not I.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] MARCELLUS. Nor I, my lord- in faith.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. Upon my sword.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] MARCELLUS. We have sworn, my lord, already.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. Indeed, upon my sword, indeed. Ghost cries under the stage.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. Aha boy, say'st thou so? Art thou there, truepenny? Come on! You hear this fellow in the cellarage. Consent to swear.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HORATIO. Propose the oath, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. Never to speak of this that you have seen. Swear by my sword.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: GHOST
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] GHOST. Swear.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. Hic et ubique? Then we'll shift our ground. Come hither, gentlemen, And lay your hands again upon my sword. Never to speak of this that you have heard: Swear by my sword.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: GHOST
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] GHOST. Swear by his sword.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. Well said, old mole! Canst work i' th' earth so fast? A worthy pioner! Once more remove, good friends."
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HORATIO. O day and night, but this is wondrous strange!
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. And therefore as a stranger give it welcome. There are more things in heaven and earth, Horatio, Than are dreamt of in your philosophy. But come! Here, as before, never, so help you mercy, How strange or odd soe'er I bear myself (As I perchance hereafter shall think meet To put an antic disposition on), That you, at such times seeing me, never shall, With arms encumb'red thus, or this head-shake, Or by pronouncing of some doubtful phrase, As 'Well, well, we know,' or 'We could, an if we would,' Or 'If we list to speak,' or 'There be, an if they might,' Or such ambiguous giving out, to note That you know aught of me- this is not to do, So grace and mercy at your most need help you, Swear.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: GHOST
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] GHOST. Swear.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.] [Keywords: revenge, Claudius] HAMLET. Rest, rest, perturbed spirit! So, gentlemen, With all my love I do commend me to you; And what so poor a man as Hamlet is May do t' express his love and friending to you, God willing, shall not lack. Let us go in together; And still your fingers on your lips, I pray. The time is out of joint. O cursed spite That ever I was born to set it right! Nay, come, let's go together.
scene_summary: The ghost says Claudius murdered him and commands revenge.
