speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Nay, do not think I flatter; For what advancement may I hope from thee, That no revenue hast but thy good spirits To feed and clothe thee? Why should the poor be flatter'd? No, let the candied tongue lick absurd pomp, And crook the pregnant hinges of the knee Where thrift may follow fawning. Dost thou hear? Since my dear soul was mistress of her choice And could of men distinguish, her election Hath seal'd thee for herself. For thou hast been As one, in suff'ring all, that suffers nothing; A man that Fortune's buffets and rewards Hast ta'en with equal thanks; and blest are those Whose blood and judgment are so well commingled That they are not a pipe for Fortune's finger To sound what stop she please. Give me that man That is not passion's slave, and I will wear him In my heart's core, ay, in my heart of heart, As I do thee. Something too much of this I There is a play to-night before the King. One scene of it comes near the circumstance, Which I have told thee, of my father's death. I prithee, when thou seest that act afoot, Even with the very comment of thy soul Observe my uncle. If his occulted guilt Do not itself unkennel in one speech, It is a damned ghost that we have seen, And my imaginations are as foul As Vulcan's stithy. Give him heedful note; For I mine eyes will rivet to his face, And after we will both our judgments join In censure of his seeming.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HORATIO. Well, my lord. If he steal aught the whilst this play is playing, And scape detecting, I will pay the theft. Sound a flourish. [Enter Trumpets and Kettledrums. Danish march. [Enter King, Queen, Polonius, Ophelia, Rosencrantz, Guildenstern, and other Lords attendant, with the Guard carrying torches.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. They are coming to the play. I must be idle. Get you a place.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] CLAUDIUS. How fares our cousin Hamlet?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Excellent, i' faith; of the chameleon's dish. I eat the air, promise-cramm'd. You cannot feed capons so.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] CLAUDIUS. I have nothing with this answer, Hamlet. These words are not mine.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. No, nor mine now. My lord, you play'd once i' th' university, you say?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] POLONIUS. That did I, my lord, and was accounted a good actor.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. What did you enact?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] POLONIUS. I did enact Julius Caesar; I was kill'd i' th' Capitol; Brutus kill'd me.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. It was a brute part of him to kill so capital a calf there. Be the players ready.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] ROSENCRANTZ. Ay, my lord. They stay upon your patience.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GERTRUDE. Come hither, my dear Hamlet, sit by me.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. No, good mother. Here's metal more attractive.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] POLONIUS. O, ho! do you mark that?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Lady, shall I lie in your lap?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] OPHELIA. No, my lord.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. I mean, my head upon your lap?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] OPHELIA. Ay, my lord.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Do you think I meant country matters?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] OPHELIA. I think nothing, my lord.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. That's a fair thought to lie between maids' legs.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] OPHELIA. What is, my lord?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] OPHELIA. You are merry, my lord.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] OPHELIA. Ay, my lord.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. O God, your only jig-maker! What should a man do but be merry? For look you how cheerfully my mother looks, and my father died within 's two hours.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] OPHELIA. Nay 'tis twice two months, my lord.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. So long? Nay then, let the devil wear black, for I'll have a suit of sables. O heavens! die two months ago, and not forgotten yet? Then there's hope a great man's memory may outlive his life half a year. But, by'r Lady, he must build churches then; or else shall he suffer not thinking on, with the hobby-horse, whose epitaph is 'For O, for O, the hobby-horse is forgot!' Hautboys play. The dumb show enters.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] OPHELIA. What means this, my lord?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Marry, this is miching malhecho; it means mischief.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] OPHELIA. Belike this show imports the argument of the play.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. We shall know by this fellow. The players cannot keep counsel; they'll tell all.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] OPHELIA. Will he tell us what this show meant?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Ay, or any show that you'll show him. Be not you asham'd to show, he'll not shame to tell you what it means.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] OPHELIA. You are naught, you are naught! I'll mark the play.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: PRO
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] PRO. For us, and for our tragedy, Here stooping to your clemency, We beg your hearing patiently.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Is this a prologue, or the posy of a ring?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] OPHELIA. 'Tis brief, my lord.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. As woman's love.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] CLAUDIUS. Full thirty times hath Phoebus' cart gone round Neptune's salt wash and Tellus' orbed ground, And thirty dozen moons with borrowed sheen About the world have times twelve thirties been, Since love our hearts, and Hymen did our hands, Unite comutual in most sacred bands.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GERTRUDE. So many journeys may the sun and moon Make us again count o'er ere love be done! But woe is me! you are so sick of late, So far from cheer and from your former state. That I distrust you. Yet, though I distrust, Discomfort you, my lord, it nothing must; For women's fear and love holds quantity, In neither aught, or in extremity. Now what my love is, proof hath made you know; And as my love is siz'd, my fear is so. Where love is great, the littlest doubts are fear; Where little fears grow great, great love grows there.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] CLAUDIUS. Faith, I must leave thee, love, and shortly too; My operant powers their functions leave to do. And thou shalt live in this fair world behind, Honour'd, belov'd, and haply one as kind For husband shalt thou-
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GERTRUDE. O, confound the rest! Such love must needs be treason in my breast. When second husband let me be accurst! None wed the second but who killed the first.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Wormwood, wormwood!
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GERTRUDE. The instances that second marriage move Are base respects of thrift, but none of love. A second time I kill my husband dead When second husband kisses me in bed.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] CLAUDIUS. I do believe you think what now you speak; But what we do determine oft we break. Purpose is but the slave to memory, Of violent birth, but poor validity; Which now, like fruit unripe, sticks on the tree, But fall unshaken when they mellow be. Most necessary 'tis that we forget To pay ourselves what to ourselves is debt. What to ourselves in passion we propose, The passion ending, doth the purpose lose. The violence of either grief or joy Their own enactures with themselves destroy. Where joy most revels, grief doth most lament; Grief joys, joy grieves, on slender accident. This world is not for aye, nor 'tis not strange That even our loves should with our fortunes change; For 'tis a question left us yet to prove, Whether love lead fortune, or else fortune love. The great man down, you mark his favourite flies, The poor advanc'd makes friends of enemies; And hitherto doth love on fortune tend, For who not needs shall never lack a friend, And who in want a hollow friend doth try, Directly seasons him his enemy. But, orderly to end where I begun, Our wills and fates do so contrary run That our devices still are overthrown; Our thoughts are ours, their ends none of our own. So think thou wilt no second husband wed; But die thy thoughts when thy first lord is dead.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GERTRUDE. Nor earth to me give food, nor heaven light, Sport and repose lock from me day and night, To desperation turn my trust and hope, An anchor's cheer in prison be my scope, Each opposite that blanks the face of joy Meet what I would have well, and it destroy, Both here and hence pursue me lasting strife, If, once a widow, ever I be wife!
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. If she should break it now!
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] CLAUDIUS. 'Tis deeply sworn. Sweet, leave me here awhile. My spirits grow dull, and fain I would beguile The tedious day with sleep.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GERTRUDE. Sleep rock thy brain, sleeps. And never come mischance between us twain!
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Madam, how like you this play?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GERTRUDE. The lady doth protest too much, methinks.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. O, but she'll keep her word.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] CLAUDIUS. Have you heard the argument? Is there no offence in't?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. No, no! They do but jest, poison in jest; no offence i' th' world.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] CLAUDIUS. What do you call the play?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. 'The Mousetrap.' Marry, how? Tropically. This play is the image of a murther done in Vienna. Gonzago is the duke's name; his wife, Baptista. You shall see anon. 'Tis a knavish piece of work; but what o' that? Your Majesty, and we that have free souls, it touches us not. Let the gall'd jade winch; our withers are unwrung.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] OPHELIA. You are as good as a chorus, my lord.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. I could interpret between you and your love, if I could see the puppets dallying.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] OPHELIA. You are keen, my lord, you are keen.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. It would cost you a groaning to take off my edge.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] OPHELIA. Still better, and worse.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. So you must take your husbands.- Begin, murtherer. Pox, leave thy damnable faces, and begin! Come, the croaking raven doth bellow for revenge.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: LUC
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] LUC. Thoughts black, hands apt, drugs fit, and time agreeing; Confederate season, else no creature seeing; Thou mixture rank, of midnight weeds collected, With Hecate's ban thrice blasted, thrice infected, Thy natural magic and dire property On wholesome life usurp immediately. Pours the poison in his ears.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. He poisons him i' th' garden for's estate. His name's Gonzago. The story is extant, and written in very choice Italian. You shall see anon how the murtherer gets the love of Gonzago's wife.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] OPHELIA. The King rises.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. What, frighted with false fire?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GERTRUDE. How fares my lord?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] POLONIUS. Give o'er the play.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] CLAUDIUS. Give me some light! Away!
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: ALL
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] ALL. Lights, lights, lights!
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Why, let the strucken deer go weep, The hart ungalled play; For some must watch, while some must sleep: Thus runs the world away. Would not this, sir, and a forest of feathers- if the rest of my fortunes turn Turk with me-with two Provincial roses on my raz'd shoes, get me a fellowship in a cry of players, sir?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HORATIO. Half a share.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. A whole one I! For thou dost know, O Damon dear, This realm dismantled was Of Jove himself; and now reigns here A very, very- pajock.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HORATIO. You might have rhym'd.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. O good Horatio, I'll take the ghost's word for a thousand pound! Didst perceive?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HORATIO. Very well, my lord.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Upon the talk of the poisoning?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HORATIO
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HORATIO. I did very well note him.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Aha! Come, some music! Come, the recorders! For if the King like not the comedy, Why then, belike he likes it not, perdy. Come, some music!
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GUILDENSTERN. Good my lord, vouchsafe me a word with you.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Sir, a whole history.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GUILDENSTERN. The King, sir-
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Ay, sir, what of him?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GUILDENSTERN. Is in his retirement, marvellous distemper'd.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. With drink, sir?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GUILDENSTERN. No, my lord; rather with choler.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Your wisdom should show itself more richer to signify this to the doctor; for me to put him to his purgation would perhaps plunge him into far more choler.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GUILDENSTERN. Good my lord, put your discourse into some frame, and start not so wildly from my affair.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. I am tame, sir; pronounce.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GUILDENSTERN. The Queen, your mother, in most great affliction of spirit hath sent me to you.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. You are welcome.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GUILDENSTERN. Nay, good my lord, this courtesy is not of the right breed. If it shall please you to make me a wholesome answer, I will do your mother's commandment; if not, your pardon and my return shall be the end of my business.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Sir, I cannot.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GUILDENSTERN. What, my lord?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Make you a wholesome answer; my wit's diseas'd. But, sir, such answer as I can make, you shall command; or rather, as you say, my mother. Therefore no more, but to the matter! My mother, you say-
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] ROSENCRANTZ. Then thus she says: your behaviour hath struck her into amazement and admiration.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. O wonderful son, that can so stonish a mother! But is there no sequel at the heels of this mother's admiration? Impart.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] ROSENCRANTZ. She desires to speak with you in her closet ere you go to bed.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. We shall obey, were she ten times our mother. Have you any further trade with us?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] ROSENCRANTZ. My lord, you once did love me.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. And do still, by these pickers and stealers!
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] ROSENCRANTZ. Good my lord, what is your cause of distemper? You do surely bar the door upon your own liberty, if you deny your griefs to your friend.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Sir, I lack advancement.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] ROSENCRANTZ. How can that be, when you have the voice of the King himself for your succession in Denmark?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Ay, sir, but 'while the grass grows'- the proverb is something musty.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GUILDENSTERN. O my lord, if my duty be too bold, my love is too unmannerly.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. I do not well understand that. Will you play upon this pipe?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GUILDENSTERN. My lord, I cannot.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. I pray you.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GUILDENSTERN. Believe me, I cannot.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. I do beseech you.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GUILDENSTERN. I know, no touch of it, my lord.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. It is as easy as lying. Govern these ventages with your fingers and thumbs, give it breath with your mouth, and it will discourse most eloquent music. Look you, these are the stops.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] GUILDENSTERN. But these cannot I command to any utt'rance of harmony. I have not the skill.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Why, look you now, how unworthy a thing you make of me! You would play upon me; you would seem to know my stops; you would pluck out the heart of my mystery; you would sound me from my lowest note to the top of my compass; and there is much music, excellent voice, in this little organ, yet cannot you make it speak. 'Sblood, do you think I am easier to be play'd on than a pipe? Call me what instrument you will, though you can fret me, you cannot play upon me.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] POLONIUS. My lord, the Queen would speak with you, and presently.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Do you see yonder cloud that's almost in shape of a camel?
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] POLONIUS. By th' mass, and 'tis like a camel indeed.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Methinks it is like a weasel.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] POLONIUS. It is back'd like a weasel.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Or like a whale.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] POLONIUS. Very like a whale.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. Then will I come to my mother by-and-by.- They fool me to the top of my bent.- I will come by-and-by.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] POLONIUS. I will say so. Exit.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.] [Keywords: play, Claudius, guilt] HAMLET. 'By-and-by' is easily said.- Leave me, friends.
scene_summary: The play-within-a-play unsettles Claudius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.] [Keywords: delay, prayer] CLAUDIUS. I like him not, nor stands it safe with us To let his madness range. Therefore prepare you; I your commission will forthwith dispatch, And he to England shall along with you. The terms of our estate may not endure Hazard so near us as doth hourly grow Out of his lunacies.
scene_summary: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.

speaker: GUILDENSTERN
text: [Scene: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.] [Keywords: delay, prayer] GUILDENSTERN. We will ourselves provide. Most holy and religious fear it is To keep those many many bodies safe That live and feed upon your Majesty.
scene_summary: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: [Scene: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.] [Keywords: delay, prayer] ROSENCRANTZ. The single and peculiar life is bound With all the strength and armour of the mind To keep itself from noyance; but much more That spirit upon whose weal depends and rests The lives of many. The cesse of majesty Dies not alone, but like a gulf doth draw What's near it with it. It is a massy wheel, Fix'd on the summit of the highest mount, To whose huge spokes ten thousand lesser things Are mortis'd and adjoin'd; which when it falls, Each small annexment, petty consequence, Attends the boist'rous ruin. Never alone Did the king sigh, but with a general groan.
scene_summary: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.] [Keywords: delay, prayer] CLAUDIUS. Arm you, I pray you, to this speedy voyage; For we will fetters put upon this fear, Which now goes too free-footed.
scene_summary: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.

speaker: BOTH
text: [Scene: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.] [Keywords: delay, prayer] BOTH. We will haste us.
scene_summary: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.] [Keywords: delay, prayer] POLONIUS. My lord, he's going to his mother's closet. Behind the arras I'll convey myself To hear the process. I'll warrant she'll tax him home; And, as you said, and wisely was it said, 'Tis meet that some more audience than a mother, Since nature makes them partial, should o'erhear The speech, of vantage. Fare you well, my liege. I'll call upon you ere you go to bed And tell you what I know.
scene_summary: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.] [Keywords: delay, prayer] CLAUDIUS. Thanks, dear my lord.
scene_summary: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.] [Keywords: delay, prayer] HAMLET. Now might I do it pat, now he is praying; And now I'll do't. And so he goes to heaven, And so am I reveng'd. That would be scann'd. A villain kills my father; and for that, I, his sole son, do this same villain send To heaven. Why, this is hire and salary, not revenge! He took my father grossly, full of bread, With all his crimes broad blown, as flush as May; And how his audit stands, who knows save heaven? But in our circumstance and course of thought, 'Tis heavy with him; and am I then reveng'd, To take him in the purging of his soul, When he is fit and seasoned for his passage? No. Up, sword, and know thou a more horrid hent. When he is drunk asleep; or in his rage; Or in th' incestuous pleasure of his bed; At gaming, swearing, or about some act That has no relish of salvation in't- Then trip him, that his heels may kick at heaven, And that his soul may be as damn'd and black As hell, whereto it goes. My mother stays. This physic but prolongs thy sickly days. Exit.
scene_summary: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.] [Keywords: delay, prayer] CLAUDIUS. My words fly up, my thoughts remain below. Words without thoughts never to heaven go. Exit.
scene_summary: Hamlet finds Claudius praying but delays killing him.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] POLONIUS. He will come straight. Look you lay home to him. Tell him his pranks have been too broad to bear with, And that your Grace hath screen'd and stood between Much heat and him. I'll silence me even here. Pray you be round with him.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. (within) Mother, mother, mother!
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. I'll warrant you; fear me not. Withdraw; I hear him coming.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. Now, mother, what's the matter?
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. Hamlet, thou hast thy father much offended.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. Mother, you have my father much offended.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. Come, come, you answer with an idle tongue.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. Go, go, you question with a wicked tongue.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. Why, how now, Hamlet?
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. What's the matter now?
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. Have you forgot me?
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. No, by the rood, not so! You are the Queen, your husband's brother's wife, And (would it were not so!) you are my mother.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. Nay, then I'll set those to you that can speak.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. Come, come, and sit you down. You shall not budge; You go not till I set you up a glass Where you may see the inmost part of you.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. What wilt thou do? Thou wilt not murther me? Help, help, ho!
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] POLONIUS. What, ho! help, help, help!
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. How now? a rat? Dead for a ducat, dead! kills Polonius.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: POLONIUS
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] POLONIUS. O, I am slain!
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. O me, what hast thou done?
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. Nay, I know not. Is it the King?
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. O, what a rash and bloody deed is this!
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. A bloody deed- almost as bad, good mother, As kill a king, and marry with his brother.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. As kill a king?
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. Ay, lady, it was my word.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. What have I done that thou dar'st wag thy tongue In noise so rude against me?
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. Such an act That blurs the grace and blush of modesty; Calls virtue hypocrite; takes off the rose From the fair forehead of an innocent love, And sets a blister there; makes marriage vows As false as dicers' oaths. O, such a deed As from the body of contraction plucks The very soul, and sweet religion makes A rhapsody of words! Heaven's face doth glow; Yea, this solidity and compound mass, With tristful visage, as against the doom, Is thought-sick at the act.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. Ah me, what act, That roars so loud and thunders in the index?
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. Look here upon th's picture, and on this, The counterfeit presentment of two brothers. See what a grace was seated on this brow; Hyperion's curls; the front of Jove himself; An eye like Mars, to threaten and command; A station like the herald Mercury New lighted on a heaven-kissing hill: A combination and a form indeed Where every god did seem to set his seal To give the world assurance of a man. This was your husband. Look you now what follows. Here is your husband, like a mildew'd ear Blasting his wholesome brother. Have you eyes? Could you on this fair mountain leave to feed, And batten on this moor? Ha! have you eyes You cannot call it love; for at your age The heyday in the blood is tame, it's humble, And waits upon the judgment; and what judgment Would step from this to this? Sense sure you have, Else could you not have motion; but sure that sense Is apoplex'd; for madness would not err, Nor sense to ecstacy was ne'er so thrall'd But it reserv'd some quantity of choice To serve in such a difference. What devil was't That thus hath cozen'd you at hoodman-blind? Eyes without feeling, feeling without sight, Ears without hands or eyes, smelling sans all, Or but a sickly part of one true sense Could not so mope. O shame! where is thy blush? Rebellious hell, If thou canst mutine in a matron's bones, To flaming youth let virtue be as wax And melt in her own fire. Proclaim no shame When the compulsive ardour gives the charge, Since frost itself as actively doth burn, And reason panders will.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. O Hamlet, speak no more! Thou turn'st mine eyes into my very soul, And there I see such black and grained spots As will not leave their tinct.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. Nay, but to live In the rank sweat of an enseamed bed, Stew'd in corruption, honeying and making love Over the nasty sty!
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. O, speak to me no more! These words like daggers enter in mine ears. No more, sweet Hamlet!
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. A murtherer and a villain! A slave that is not twentieth part the tithe Of your precedent lord; a vice of kings; A cutpurse of the empire and the rule, That from a shelf the precious diadem stole And put it in his pocket!
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. A king of shreds and patches!- Save me and hover o'er me with your wings, You heavenly guards! What would your gracious figure?
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. Alas, he's mad!
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. Do you not come your tardy son to chide, That, laps'd in time and passion, lets go by Th' important acting of your dread command? O, say!
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GHOST
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GHOST. Do not forget. This visitation Is but to whet thy almost blunted purpose. But look, amazement on thy mother sits. O, step between her and her fighting soul Conceit in weakest bodies strongest works. Speak to her, Hamlet.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. How is it with you, lady?
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. Alas, how is't with you, That you do bend your eye on vacancy, And with th' encorporal air do hold discourse? Forth at your eyes your spirits wildly peep; And, as the sleeping soldiers in th' alarm, Your bedded hairs, like life in excrements, Start up and stand an end. O gentle son, Upon the heat and flame of thy distemper Sprinkle cool patience! Whereon do you look?
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. On him, on him! Look you how pale he glares! His form and cause conjoin'd, preaching to stones, Would make them capable.- Do not look upon me, Lest with this piteous action you convert My stern effects. Then what I have to do Will want true colour- tears perchance for blood.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. To whom do you speak this?

text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. Why, look you there! Look how it steals away! My father, in his habit as he liv'd! Look where he goes even now out at the portal!

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. This is the very coinage of your brain. This bodiless creation ecstasy Is very cunning in.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. Ecstasy? My pulse as yours doth temperately keep time And makes as healthful music. It is not madness That I have utt'red. Bring me to the test, And I the matter will reword; which madness Would gambol from. Mother, for love of grace, Lay not that flattering unction to your soul That not your trespass but my madness speaks. It will but skin and film the ulcerous place, Whiles rank corruption, mining all within, Infects unseen. Confess yourself to heaven; Repent what's past; avoid what is to come; And do not spread the compost on the weeds To make them ranker. Forgive me this my virtue; For in the fatness of these pursy times Virtue itself of vice must pardon beg- Yea, curb and woo for leave to do him good.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. O Hamlet, thou hast cleft my heart in twain.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. O, throw away the worser part of it, And live the purer with the other half, Good night- but go not to my uncle's bed. Assume a virtue, if you have it not. That monster, custom, who all sense doth eat Of habits evil, is angel yet in this, That to the use of actions fair and good He likewise gives a frock or livery, That aptly is put on. Refrain to-night, And that shall lend a kind of easiness To the next abstinence; the next more easy; For use almost can change the stamp of nature, And either the devil, or throw him out With wondrous potency. Once more, good night; And when you are desirous to be blest, I'll blessing beg of you.- For this same lord, I do repent; but heaven hath pleas'd it so, To punish me with this, and this with me, That I must be their scourge and minister. I will bestow him, and will answer well The death I gave him. So again, good night. I must be cruel, only to be kind; Thus bad begins, and worse remains behind. One word more, good lady.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. What shall I do?
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. Not this, by no means, that I bid you do: Let the bloat King tempt you again to bed; Pinch wanton on your cheek; call you his mouse; And let him, for a pair of reechy kisses, Or paddling in your neck with his damn'd fingers, Make you to ravel all this matter out, That I essentially am not in madness, But mad in craft. 'Twere good you let him know; For who that's but a queen, fair, sober, wise, Would from a paddock, from a bat, a gib Such dear concernings hide? Who would do so? No, in despite of sense and secrecy, Unpeg the basket on the house's top, Let the birds fly, and like the famous ape, To try conclusions, in the basket creep And break your own neck down.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. Be thou assur'd, if words be made of breath, And breath of life, I have no life to breathe What thou hast said to me.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. I must to England; you know that?
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] GERTRUDE. Alack, I had forgot! 'Tis so concluded on.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Polonius] HAMLET. There's letters seal'd; and my two schoolfellows, Whom I will trust as I will adders fang'd, They bear the mandate; they must sweep my way And marshal me to knavery. Let it work; For 'tis the sport to have the enginer Hoist with his own petar; and 't shall go hard But I will delve one yard below their mines And blow them at the moon. O, 'tis most sweet When in one line two crafts directly meet. This man shall set me packing. I'll lug the guts into the neighbour room.- Mother, good night.- Indeed, this counsellor Is now most still, most secret, and most grave, Who was in life a foolish peating knave. Come, sir, to draw toward an end with you. Good night, mother.
scene_summary: Hamlet confronts Gertrude and kills Polonius behind the arras.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Gertrude tells Claudius that Hamlet killed Polonius.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Claudius] GERTRUDE. Mad as the sea and wind when both contend Which is the mightier. In his lawless fit Behind the arras hearing something stir, Whips out his rapier, cries 'A rat, a rat!' And in this brainish apprehension kills The unseen good old man.
scene_summary: Gertrude tells Claudius that Hamlet killed Polonius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: Gertrude tells Claudius that Hamlet killed Polonius.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Claudius] CLAUDIUS. O heavy deed! It had been so with us, had we been there. His liberty is full of threats to all- To you yourself, to us, to every one. Alas, how shall this bloody deed be answer'd? It will be laid to us, whose providence Should have kept short, restrain'd, and out of haunt This mad young man. But so much was our love We would not understand what was most fit, But, like the owner of a foul disease, To keep it from divulging, let it feed Even on the pith of life. Where is he gone?
scene_summary: Gertrude tells Claudius that Hamlet killed Polonius.

speaker: GERTRUDE
text: [Scene: Gertrude tells Claudius that Hamlet killed Polonius.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Claudius] GERTRUDE. To draw apart the body he hath kill'd; O'er whom his very madness, like some ore Among a mineral of metals base, Shows itself pure. He weeps for what is done.
scene_summary: Gertrude tells Claudius that Hamlet killed Polonius.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: Gertrude tells Claudius that Hamlet killed Polonius.] [Keywords: Gertrude, Claudius] CLAUDIUS. O Gertrude, come away! The sun no sooner shall the mountains touch But we will ship him hence; and this vile deed We must with all our majesty and skill Both countenance and excuse. Ho, Guildenstern!
scene_summary: Gertrude tells Claudius that Hamlet killed Polonius.

text: [Scene: Hamlet refuses to reveal where Polonius’s body is.] [Keywords: body, Polonius] HAMLET. That I can keep your counsel, and not mine own. Besides, to be demanded of a sponge, what replication should be made by the son of a king?

text: [Scene: Hamlet refuses to reveal where Polonius’s body is.] [Keywords: body, Polonius] HAMLET. Ay, sir; that soaks up the King's countenance, his rewards, his authorities. But such officers do the King best service in the end. He keeps them, like an ape, in the corner of his jaw; first mouth'd, to be last swallowed. When he needs what you have glean'd, it is but squeezing you and, sponge, you shall be dry again.

speaker: CLAUDIUS
text: [Scene: Claudius sends Hamlet to England with secret orders for his death.] [Keywords: England, plot] CLAUDIUS. I have sent to seek him and to find the body. How dangerous is it that this man goes loose! Yet must not we put the strong law on him. He's lov'd of the distracted multitude, Who like not in their judgment, but their eyes; And where 'tis so, th' offender's scourge is weigh'd, But never the offence. To bear all smooth and even, This sudden sending him away must seem Deliberate pause. Diseases desperate grown By desperate appliance are reliev'd, Or not at all.
scene_summary: Claudius sends Hamlet to England with secret orders for his death.

text: [Scene: Claudius sends Hamlet to England with secret orders for his death.] [Keywords: England, plot] ROSENCRANTZ. Where the dead body is bestow'd, my lord, We cannot get from him.

text: [Scene: Claudius sends Hamlet to England with secret orders for his death.] [Keywords: England, plot] HAMLET. Not where he eats, but where he is eaten. A certain convocation of politic worms are e'en at him. Your worm is your only emperor for diet. We fat all creatures else to fat us, and we fat ourselves for maggots. Your fat king and your lean beggar is but variable service- two dishes, but to one table. That's the end.

The bark is ready and the wind at help, Th' associates tend, and everything is bent For England.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet compares himself to Fortinbras and criticises his delay.] [Keywords: Fortinbras, delay] HAMLET. Goes it against the main of Poland, sir, Or for some frontier?
scene_summary: Hamlet compares himself to Fortinbras and criticises his delay.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: [Scene: Hamlet compares himself to Fortinbras and criticises his delay.] [Keywords: Fortinbras, delay] CAPTAIN. Truly to speak, and with no addition, We go to gain a little patch of ground That hath in it no profit but the name. To pay five ducats, five, I would not farm it; Nor will it yield to Norway or the Pole A ranker rate, should it be sold in fee.
scene_summary: Hamlet compares himself to Fortinbras and criticises his delay.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet compares himself to Fortinbras and criticises his delay.] [Keywords: Fortinbras, delay] HAMLET. Why, then the Polack never will defend it.
scene_summary: Hamlet compares himself to Fortinbras and criticises his delay.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: [Scene: Hamlet compares himself to Fortinbras and criticises his delay.] [Keywords: Fortinbras, delay] CAPTAIN. Yes, it is already garrison'd.
scene_summary: Hamlet compares himself to Fortinbras and criticises his delay.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet compares himself to Fortinbras and criticises his delay.] [Keywords: Fortinbras, delay] HAMLET. Two thousand souls and twenty thousand ducats Will not debate the question of this straw. This is th' imposthume of much wealth and peace, That inward breaks, and shows no cause without Why the man dies.- I humbly thank you, sir.
scene_summary: Hamlet compares himself to Fortinbras and criticises his delay.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: [Scene: Hamlet compares himself to Fortinbras and criticises his delay.] [Keywords: Fortinbras, delay] CAPTAIN. God b' wi' you, sir.
scene_summary: Hamlet compares himself to Fortinbras and criticises his delay.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: [Scene: Hamlet compares himself to Fortinbras and criticises his delay.] [Keywords: Fortinbras, delay] ROSENCRANTZ. Will't please you go, my lord?
scene_summary: Hamlet compares himself to Fortinbras and criticises his delay.

speaker: HAMLET
text: [Scene: Hamlet compares himself to Fortinbras and criticises his delay.] [Keywords: Fortinbras, delay] HAMLET. I'll be with you straight. Go a little before.
scene_summary: Hamlet compares himself to Fortinbras and criticises his delay.
