speaker: VERONA
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] VERONA. A public place.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. Gregory, on my word, we'll not carry coals.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: GREGORY
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] GREGORY. No, for then we should be colliers.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. I mean, an we be in choler, we'll draw.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: GREGORY
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] GREGORY. Ay, while you live, draw your neck out of collar.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. I strike quickly, being moved.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: GREGORY
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] GREGORY. But thou art not quickly moved to strike.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. A dog of the house of Montague moves me.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: GREGORY
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] GREGORY. To move is to stir, and to be valiant is to stand. Therefore, if thou art moved, thou runn'st away.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. A dog of that house shall move me to stand. I will take the wall of any man or maid of Montague's.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: GREGORY
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] GREGORY. That shows thee a weak slave; for the weakest goes to the wall.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. 'Tis true; and therefore women, being the weaker vessels, are ever thrust to the wall. Therefore I will push Montague's men from the wall and thrust his maids to the wall.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: GREGORY
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] GREGORY. The quarrel is between our masters and us their men.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. 'Tis all one. I will show myself a tyrant. When I have fought with the men, I will be cruel with the maids- I will cut off their heads.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: GREGORY
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] GREGORY. The heads of the maids?
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. Ay, the heads of the maids, or their maidenheads. Take it in what sense thou wilt.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: GREGORY
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] GREGORY. They must take it in sense that feel it.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. Me they shall feel while I am able to stand; and 'tis known I am a pretty piece of flesh.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: GREGORY
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] GREGORY. 'Tis well thou art not fish; if thou hadst, thou hadst been poor-John. Draw thy tool! Here comes two of the house of Montagues.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. My naked weapon is out. Quarrel! I will back thee.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: GREGORY
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] GREGORY. How? turn thy back and run?
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. Fear me not.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: GREGORY
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] GREGORY. No, marry. I fear thee!
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. Let us take the law of our sides; let them begin.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: GREGORY
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] GREGORY. I will frown as I pass by, and let them take it as they list.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. Nay, as they dare. I will bite my thumb at them; which is disgrace to them, if they bear it.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: ABRAM
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ABRAM. Do you bite your thumb at us, sir?
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. I do bite my thumb, sir.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: ABRAM
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ABRAM. Do you bite your thumb at us, sir?
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. Is the law of our side if I say ay?
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: GREGORY
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] GREGORY. No.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. No, sir, I do not bite my thumb at you, sir; but I bite my thumb, sir.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: GREGORY
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] GREGORY. Do you quarrel, sir?
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: ABRAM
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ABRAM. Quarrel, sir? No, sir.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. But if you do, sir, am for you. I serve as good a man as you.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: ABRAM
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ABRAM. No better.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. Well, sir.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: GREGORY
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] GREGORY. Say 'better.' Here comes one of my master's kinsmen.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. Yes, better, sir.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: SAMPSON
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] SAMPSON. Draw, if you be men. Gregory, remember thy swashing blow. They fight.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. Part, fools! Put up your swords. You know not what you do.

speaker: TYBALT
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] TYBALT. What, art thou drawn among these heartless hinds? Turn thee Benvolio! look upon thy death.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. I do but keep the peace. Put up thy sword, Or manage it to part these men with me.

speaker: TYBALT
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] TYBALT. What, drawn, and talk of peace? I hate the word As I hate hell, all Montagues, and thee. Have at thee, coward! They fight.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: OFFICER
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] OFFICER. Clubs, bills, and partisans! Strike! beat them down!

speaker: CITIZENS
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] CITIZENS. Down with the Capulets! Down with the Montagues!

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] CAPULET. My sword, I say! Old Montague is come And flourishes his blade in spite of me.

text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] MONTAGUE. Thou villain Capulet!- Hold me not, let me go.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: M
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] M. Wife. Thou shalt not stir one foot to seek a foe.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: PRINCE
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] PRINCE. Rebellious subjects, enemies to peace, Profaners of this neighbour-stained steel- Will they not hear? What, ho! you men, you beasts, That quench the fire of your pernicious rage With purple fountains issuing from your veins! On pain of torture, from those bloody hands Throw your mistempered weapons to the ground And hear the sentence of your moved prince. Three civil brawls, bred of an airy word By thee, old Capulet, and Montague, Have thrice disturb'd the quiet of our streets And made Verona's ancient citizens Cast by their grave beseeming ornaments To wield old partisans, in hands as old, Cank'red with peace, to part your cank'red hate. If ever you disturb our streets again, Your lives shall pay the forfeit of the peace. For this time all the rest depart away. You, Capulet, shall go along with me; And, Montague, come you this afternoon, To know our farther pleasure in this case, To old Freetown, our common judgment place. Once more, on pain of death, all men depart.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] MONTAGUE. Who set this ancient quarrel new abroach? Speak, nephew, were you by when it began?
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. Here were the servants of your adversary And yours, close fighting ere I did approach. I drew to part them. In the instant came The fiery Tybalt, with his sword prepar'd; Which, as he breath'd defiance to my ears, He swung about his head and cut the winds, Who, nothing hurt withal, hiss'd him in scorn. While we were interchanging thrusts and blows, Came more and more, and fought on part and part, Till the Prince came, who parted either part.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: M
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] M. Wife. O, where is Romeo? Saw you him to-day? Right glad I am he was not at this fray.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. Madam, an hour before the worshipp'd sun Peer'd forth the golden window of the East, A troubled mind drave me to walk abroad; Where, underneath the grove of sycamore That westward rooteth from the city's side, So early walking did I see your son. Towards him I made; but he was ware of me And stole into the covert of the wood. I- measuring his affections by my own, Which then most sought where most might not be found, Being one too many by my weary self- Pursu'd my humour, not Pursuing his, And gladly shunn'd who gladly fled from me.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] MONTAGUE. Many a morning hath he there been seen, With tears augmenting the fresh morning's dew, Adding to clouds more clouds with his deep sighs; But all so soon as the all-cheering sun Should in the farthest East bean to draw The shady curtains from Aurora's bed, Away from light steals home my heavy son And private in his chamber pens himself, Shuts up his windows, locks fair daylight out And makes himself an artificial night. Black and portentous must this humour prove Unless good counsel may the cause remove.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. My noble uncle, do you know the cause?
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] MONTAGUE. I neither know it nor can learn of him.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. Have you importun'd him by any means?
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] MONTAGUE. Both by myself and many other friends; But he, his own affections' counsellor, Is to himself- I will not say how true- But to himself so secret and so close, So far from sounding and discovery, As is the bud bit with an envious worm Ere he can spread his sweet leaves to the air Or dedicate his beauty to the sun. Could we but learn from whence his sorrows grow, We would as willingly give cure as know.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. See, where he comes. So please you step aside, I'll know his grievance, or be much denied.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] MONTAGUE. I would thou wert so happy by thy stay To hear true shrift. Come, madam, let's away,

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. Good morrow, cousin.

text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ROMEO. Is the day so young?

text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. But new struck nine.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ROMEO. Ah me! sad hours seem long. Was that my father that went hence so fast?

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. It was. What sadness lengthens Romeo's hours?
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ROMEO. Not having that which having makes them short.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ROMEO. Out of her favour where I am in love.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. Alas that love, so gentle in his view, Should be so tyrannous and rough in proof!
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ROMEO. Alas that love, whose view is muffled still, Should without eyes see pathways to his will! Where shall we dine? O me! What fray was here? Yet tell me not, for I have heard it all. Here's much to do with hate, but more with love. Why then, O brawling love! O loving hate! O anything, of nothing first create! O heavy lightness! serious vanity! Misshapen chaos of well-seeming forms! Feather of lead, bright smoke, cold fire, sick health! Still-waking sleep, that is not what it is This love feel I, that feel no love in this. Dost thou not laugh?
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. No, coz, I rather weep.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ROMEO. Good heart, at what?
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. At thy good heart's oppression.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ROMEO. Why, such is love's transgression. Griefs of mine own lie heavy in my breast, Which thou wilt propagate, to have it prest With more of thine. This love that thou hast shown Doth add more grief to too much of mine own. Love is a smoke rais'd with the fume of sighs; Being purg'd, a fire sparkling in lovers' eyes; Being vex'd, a sea nourish'd with lovers' tears. What is it else? A madness most discreet, A choking gall, and a preserving sweet. Farewell, my coz.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. Soft! I will go along. An if you leave me so, you do me wrong.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ROMEO. Tut! I have lost myself; I am not here: This is not Romeo, he's some other where.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. Tell me in sadness, who is that you love?
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ROMEO. What, shall I groan and tell thee?
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. Groan? Why, no; But sadly tell me who.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ROMEO. Bid a sick man in sadness make his will. Ah, word ill urg'd to one that is so ill! In sadness, cousin, I do love a woman.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. I aim'd so near when I suppos'd you lov'd.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ROMEO. A right good markman! And she's fair I love.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. A right fair mark, fair coz, is soonest hit.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ROMEO. Well, in that hit you miss. She'll not be hit With Cupid's arrow. She hath Dian's wit, And, in strong proof of chastity well arm'd, From Love's weak childish bow she lives unharm'd. She will not stay the siege of loving terms, Nor bide th' encounter of assailing eyes, Nor ope her lap to saint-seducing gold. O, she's rich in beauty; only poor That, when she dies, with beauty dies her store.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. Then she hath sworn that she will still live chaste?
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ROMEO. She hath, and in that sparing makes huge waste; For beauty, starv'd with her severity, Cuts beauty off from all posterity. She is too fair, too wise, wisely too fair, To merit bliss by making me despair. She hath forsworn to love, and in that vow Do I live dead that live to tell it now.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. Be rul'd by me: forget to think of her.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ROMEO. O, teach me how I should forget to think!
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. By giving liberty unto thine eyes. Examine other beauties.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] ROMEO. 'Tis the way To call hers (exquisite) in question more. These happy masks that kiss fair ladies' brows, Being black puts us in mind they hide the fair. He that is strucken blind cannot forget The precious treasure of his eyesight lost. Show me a mistress that is passing fair, What doth her beauty serve but as a note Where I may read who pass'd that passing fair?
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: FAREWELL
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] FAREWELL. Thou canst not teach me to forget.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.] [Keywords: feud, Romeo, Rosaline] BENVOLIO. I'll pay that doctrine, or else die in debt. Exeunt.
scene_summary: A street fight shows the feud; Romeo is lovesick for Rosaline.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] CAPULET. But Montague is bound as well as I, In penalty alike; and 'tis not hard, I think, For men so old as we to keep the peace.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: PAR
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] PAR. Of honourable reckoning are you both, And pity 'tis you liv'd at odds so long. But now, my lord, what say you to my suit?
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] CAPULET. But saying o'er what I have said before: My child is yet a stranger in the world, She hath not seen the change of fourteen years; Let two more summers wither in their pride Ere we may think her ripe to be a bride.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: PAR
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] PAR. Younger than she are happy mothers made.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] CAPULET. And too soon marr'd are those so early made. The earth hath swallowed all my hopes but she; She is the hopeful lady of my earth. But woo her, gentle Paris, get her heart; My will to her consent is but a part. An she agree, within her scope of choice Lies my consent and fair according voice. This night I hold an old accustom'd feast, Whereto I have invited many a guest, Such as I love; and you among the store, One more, most welcome, makes my number more. At my poor house look to behold this night Earth-treading stars that make dark heaven light. Such comfort as do lusty young men feel When well apparell'd April on the heel Of limping Winter treads, even such delight Among fresh female buds shall you this night Inherit at my house. Hear all, all see, And like her most whose merit most shall be; Which, on more view of many, mine, being one, May stand in number, though in reck'ning none. Come, go with me. Go, sirrah, trudge about Through fair Verona; find those persons out Whose names are written there, and to them say, My house and welcome on their pleasure stay-
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: SERV
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] SERV. Find them out whose names are written here? It is written that the shoemaker should meddle with his yard and the tailor with his last, the fisher with his pencil and the painter with his nets; but I am sent to find those persons whose names are here writ, and can never find what names the writing person hath here writ. I must to the learned. In good time!
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] BENVOLIO. Tut, man, one fire burns out another's burning; One pain is lessened by another's anguish; Turn giddy, and be holp by backward turning; One desperate grief cures with another's languish. Take thou some new infection to thy eye, And the rank poison of the old will die.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] ROMEO. Your plantain leaf is excellent for that.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] BENVOLIO. For what, I pray thee?
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] ROMEO. For your broken shin.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] BENVOLIO. Why, Romeo, art thou mad?
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] ROMEO. Not mad, but bound more than a madman is; Shut up in prison, kept without my food, Whipp'd and tormented and- God-den, good fellow.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: SERV
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] SERV. God gi' go-den. I pray, sir, can you read?
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] ROMEO. Ay, mine own fortune in my misery.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: SERV
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] SERV. Perhaps you have learned it without book. But I pray, can you read anything you see?
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] ROMEO. Ay, If I know the letters and the language.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: SERV
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] SERV. Ye say honestly. Rest you merry!
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] ROMEO. Stay, fellow; I can read. He reads. 'Signior Martino and his wife and daughters; County Anselmo and his beauteous sisters; The lady widow of Vitruvio; Signior Placentio and His lovely nieces; Mercutio and his brother Valentine; Mine uncle Capulet, his wife, and daughters; My fair niece Rosaline and Livia; Signior Valentio and his cousin Tybalt; Lucio and the lively Helena.' A fair assembly. Whither should they come?
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: SERV
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] SERV. To supper, to our house.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] ROMEO. Whose house?
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: SERV
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] SERV. My master's.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] ROMEO. Indeed I should have ask'd you that before.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: SERV
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] SERV. Now I'll tell you without asking. My master is the great rich Capulet; and if you be not of the house of Montagues, I pray come and crush a cup of wine. Rest you merry! Exit.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] BENVOLIO. At this same ancient feast of Capulet's Sups the fair Rosaline whom thou so lov'st; With all the admired beauties of Verona. Go thither, and with unattainted eye Compare her face with some that I shall show, And I will make thee think thy swan a crow.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] ROMEO. When the devout religion of mine eye Maintains such falsehood, then turn tears to fires; And these, who, often drown'd, could never die, Transparent heretics, be burnt for liars! One fairer than my love? The all-seeing sun Ne'er saw her match since first the world begun.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] BENVOLIO. Tut! you saw her fair, none else being by, Herself pois'd with herself in either eye; But in that crystal scales let there be weigh'd Your lady's love against some other maid That I will show you shining at this feast, And she shall scant show well that now seems best.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.] [Keywords: Paris, Juliet, feast] ROMEO. I'll go along, no such sight to be shown, But to rejoice in splendour of my own.
scene_summary: Paris asks to marry Juliet, while Romeo learns about the Capulet feast.

speaker: WIFE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] WIFE. Nurse, where's my daughter? Call her forth to me.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] NURSE. Now, by my maidenhead at twelve year old, I bade her come. What, lamb! what ladybird! God forbid! Where's this girl? What, Juliet!
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] JULIET. How now? Who calls?
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] NURSE. Your mother.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] JULIET. Madam, I am here. What is your will?
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: WIFE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] WIFE. This is the matter- Nurse, give leave awhile, We must talk in secret. Nurse, come back again; I have rememb'red me, thou's hear our counsel. Thou knowest my daughter's of a pretty age.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] NURSE. Faith, I can tell her age unto an hour.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: WIFE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] WIFE. She's not fourteen.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] NURSE. I'll lay fourteen of my teeth- And yet, to my teen be it spoken, I have but four- She is not fourteen. How long is it now To Lammastide?
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: WIFE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] WIFE. A fortnight and odd days.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] NURSE. Even or odd, of all days in the year, Come Lammas Eve at night shall she be fourteen. Susan and she (God rest all Christian souls!) Were of an age. Well, Susan is with God; She was too good for me. But, as I said, On Lammas Eve at night shall she be fourteen; That shall she, marry; I remember it well. 'Tis since the earthquake now eleven years; And she was wean'd (I never shall forget it), Of all the days of the year, upon that day; For I had then laid wormwood to my dug, Sitting in the sun under the dovehouse wall. My lord and you were then at Mantua. Nay, I do bear a brain. But, as I said, When it did taste the wormwood on the nipple Of my dug and felt it bitter, pretty fool, To see it tetchy and fall out with the dug! Shake, quoth the dovehouse! 'Twas no need, I trow, To bid me trudge. And since that time it is eleven years, For then she could stand alone; nay, by th' rood, She could have run and waddled all about; For even the day before, she broke her brow; And then my husband (God be with his soul! 'A was a merry man) took up the child. 'Yea,' quoth he, 'dost thou fall upon thy face? Thou wilt fall backward when thou hast more wit; Wilt thou not, Jule?' and, by my holidam, The pretty wretch left crying, and said 'Ay.' To see now how a jest shall come about! I warrant, an I should live a thousand yeas, I never should forget it. 'Wilt thou not, Jule?' quoth he, And, pretty fool, it stinted, and said 'Ay.'
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: WIFE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] WIFE. Enough of this. I pray thee hold thy peace.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] NURSE. Yes, madam. Yet I cannot choose but laugh To think it should leave crying and say 'Ay.' And yet, I warrant, it had upon its brow A bump as big as a young cock'rel's stone; A perilous knock; and it cried bitterly. 'Yea,' quoth my husband, 'fall'st upon thy face? Thou wilt fall backward when thou comest to age; Wilt thou not, Jule?' It stinted, and said 'Ay.'
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] JULIET. And stint thou too, I pray thee, nurse, say I.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] NURSE. Peace, I have done. God mark thee to his grace! Thou wast the prettiest babe that e'er I nurs'd. An I might live to see thee married once, I have my wish.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: WIFE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] WIFE. Marry, that 'marry' is the very theme I came to talk of. Tell me, daughter Juliet, How stands your disposition to be married?
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] JULIET. It is an honour that I dream not of.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] NURSE. An honour? Were not I thine only nurse, I would say thou hadst suck'd wisdom from thy teat.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: WIFE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] WIFE. Well, think of marriage now. Younger than you, Here in Verona, ladies of esteem, Are made already mothers. By my count, I was your mother much upon these years That you are now a maid. Thus then in brief: The valiant Paris seeks you for his love.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] NURSE. A man, young lady! lady, such a man As all the world- why he's a man of wax.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: WIFE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] WIFE. Verona's summer hath not such a flower.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] NURSE. Nay, he's a flower, in faith- a very flower.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: WIFE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] WIFE. What say you? Can you love the gentleman? This night you shall behold him at our feast. Read o'er the volume of young Paris' face, And find delight writ there with beauty's pen; Examine every married lineament, And see how one another lends content; And what obscur'd in this fair volume lies Find written in the margent of his eyes, This precious book of love, this unbound lover, To beautify him only lacks a cover. The fish lives in the sea, and 'tis much pride For fair without the fair within to hide. That book in many's eyes doth share the glory, That in gold clasps locks in the golden story; So shall you share all that he doth possess, By having him making yourself no less.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] NURSE. No less? Nay, bigger! Women grow by men
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: WIFE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] WIFE. Speak briefly, can you like of Paris' love?
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] JULIET. I'll look to like, if looking liking move; But no more deep will I endart mine eye Than your consent gives strength to make it fly.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: SERV
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] SERV. Madam, the guests are come, supper serv'd up, you call'd, my young lady ask'd for, the nurse curs'd in the pantry, and everything in extremity. I must hence to wait. I beseech you follow straight.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: WIFE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] WIFE. We follow thee. Exit . Juliet, the County stays.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.] [Keywords: Juliet, marriage, Nurse] NURSE. Go, girl, seek happy nights to happy days.
scene_summary: Lady Capulet and the Nurse discuss Paris as a husband for Juliet.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] ROMEO. What, shall this speech be spoke for our excuse? Or shall we on without apology?
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] BENVOLIO. The date is out of such prolixity. We'll have no Cupid hoodwink'd with a scarf, Bearing a Tartar's painted bow of lath, Scaring the ladies like a crowkeeper; Nor no without-book prologue, faintly spoke After the prompter, for our entrance; But, let them measure us by what they will, We'll measure them a measure, and be gone.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] ROMEO. Give me a torch. I am not for this ambling. Being but heavy, I will bear the light.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] MERCUTIO. Nay, gentle Romeo, we must have you dance.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] ROMEO. Not I, believe me. You have dancing shoes With nimble soles; I have a soul of lead So stakes me to the ground I cannot move.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] MERCUTIO. You are a lover. Borrow Cupid's wings And soar with them above a common bound.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] ROMEO. I am too sore enpierced with his shaft To soar with his light feathers; and so bound I cannot bound a pitch above dull woe. Under love's heavy burthen do I sink.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] MERCUTIO. And, to sink in it, should you burthen love- Too great oppression for a tender thing.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] ROMEO. Is love a tender thing? It is too rough, Too rude, too boist'rous, and it pricks like thorn.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] MERCUTIO. If love be rough with you, be rough with love. Prick love for pricking, and you beat love down. Give me a case to put my visage in. A visor for a visor! What care I What curious eye doth quote deformities? Here are the beetle brows shall blush for me.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] BENVOLIO. Come, knock and enter; and no sooner in But every man betake him to his legs.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] ROMEO. A torch for me! Let wantons light of heart Tickle the senseless rushes with their heels; For I am proverb'd with a grandsire phrase, I'll be a candle-holder and look on; The game was ne'er so fair, and I am done.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] MERCUTIO. Tut! dun's the mouse, the constable's own word! If thou art Dun, we'll draw thee from the mire Of this sir-reverence love, wherein thou stick'st Up to the ears. Come, we burn daylight, ho!
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] ROMEO. Nay, that's not so.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] MERCUTIO. I mean, sir, in delay We waste our lights in vain, like lamps by day. Take our good meaning, for our judgment sits Five times in that ere once in our five wits.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] ROMEO. And we mean well, in going to this masque; But 'tis no wit to go.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] MERCUTIO. Why, may one ask?
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] ROMEO. I dreamt a dream to-night.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] MERCUTIO. And so did I.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] ROMEO. Well, what was yours?
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] MERCUTIO. That dreamers often lie.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] ROMEO. In bed asleep, while they do dream things true.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] MERCUTIO. O, then I see Queen Mab hath been with you. She is the fairies' midwife, and she comes In shape no bigger than an agate stone On the forefinger of an alderman, Drawn with a team of little atomies Athwart men's noses as they lie asleep; Her wagon spokes made of long spinners' legs, The cover, of the wings of grasshoppers; Her traces, of the smallest spider's web; Her collars, of the moonshine's wat'ry beams; Her whip, of cricket's bone; the lash, of film; Her wagoner, a small grey-coated gnat, Not half so big as a round little worm Prick'd from the lazy finger of a maid; Her chariot is an empty hazelnut, Made by the joiner squirrel or old grub, Time out o' mind the fairies' coachmakers. And in this state she gallops night by night Through lovers' brains, and then they dream of love; O'er courtiers' knees, that dream on cursies straight; O'er lawyers' fingers, who straight dream on fees; O'er ladies' lips, who straight on kisses dream, Which oft the angry Mab with blisters plagues, Because their breaths with sweetmeats tainted are. Sometime she gallops o'er a courtier's nose, And then dreams he of smelling out a suit; And sometime comes she with a tithe-pig's tail Tickling a parson's nose as 'a lies asleep, Then dreams he of another benefice. Sometimes she driveth o'er a soldier's neck, And then dreams he of cutting foreign throats, Of breaches, ambuscadoes, Spanish blades, Of healths five fadom deep; and then anon Drums in his ear, at which he starts and wakes, And being thus frighted, swears a prayer or two And sleeps again. This is that very Mab That plats the manes of horses in the night And bakes the elflocks in foul sluttish, hairs, Which once untangled much misfortune bodes This is the hag, when maids lie on their backs, That presses them and learns them first to bear, Making them women of good carriage. This is she-
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] ROMEO. Peace, peace, Mercutio, peace! Thou talk'st of nothing.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] MERCUTIO. True, I talk of dreams; Which are the children of an idle brain, Begot of nothing but vain fantasy; Which is as thin of substance as the air, And more inconstant than the wind, who wooes Even now the frozen bosom of the North And, being anger'd, puffs away from thence, Turning his face to the dew-dropping South.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] BENVOLIO. This wind you talk of blows us from ourselves. Supper is done, and we shall come too late.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] ROMEO. I fear, too early; for my mind misgives Some consequence, yet hanging in the stars, Shall bitterly begin his fearful date With this night's revels and expire the term Of a despised life, clos'd in my breast, By some vile forfeit of untimely death. But he that hath the steerage of my course Direct my sail! On, lusty gentlemen!
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.] [Keywords: Mercutio, dreams] BENVOLIO. Strike, drum. They march about the stage.
scene_summary: Romeo and friends prepare for the Capulet party; Mercutio mocks dreams.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love before learning they are from enemy families.] [Keywords: meeting, love, feud] CAPULET. Welcome, gentlemen! Ladies that have their toes Unplagu'd with corns will have a bout with you. Ah ha, my mistresses! which of you all Will now deny to dance? She that makes dainty, She I'll swear hath corns. Am I come near ye now? Welcome, gentlemen! I have seen the day That I have worn a visor and could tell A whispering tale in a fair lady's ear, Such as would please. 'Tis gone, 'tis gone, 'tis gone! You are welcome, gentlemen! Come, musicians, play. A hall, a hall! give room! and foot it, girls. Music plays, and they dance. More light, you knaves! and turn the tables up, And quench the fire, the room is grown too hot. Ah, sirrah, this unlook'd-for sport comes well. Nay, sit, nay, sit, good cousin Capulet, For you and I are past our dancing days. How long is't now since last yourself and I Were in a mask? 2. Cap. By'r Lady, thirty years.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love before learning they are from enemy families.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love before learning they are from enemy families.] [Keywords: meeting, love, feud] CAPULET. What, man? 'Tis not so much, 'tis not so much! 'Tis since the nuptial of Lucentio, Come Pentecost as quickly as it will, Some five-and-twenty years, and then we mask'd. 2. Cap. 'Tis more, 'tis more! His son is elder, sir; His son is thirty.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love before learning they are from enemy families.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love before learning they are from enemy families.] [Keywords: meeting, love, feud] CAPULET. Will you tell me that? His son was but a ward two years ago.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love before learning they are from enemy families.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love before learning they are from enemy families.] [Keywords: meeting, love, feud] ROMEO. What lady's that, which doth enrich the hand Of yonder knight?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love before learning they are from enemy families.

speaker: SERV
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love before learning they are from enemy families.] [Keywords: meeting, love, feud] SERV. I know not, sir.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love before learning they are from enemy families.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love before learning they are from enemy families.] [Keywords: meeting, love, feud] ROMEO. O, she doth teach the torches to burn bright! It seems she hangs upon the cheek of night Like a rich jewel in an Ethiop's ear- Beauty too rich for use, for earth too dear! So shows a snowy dove trooping with crows As yonder lady o'er her fellows shows. The measure done, I'll watch her place of stand And, touching hers, make blessed my rude hand. Did my heart love till now? Forswear it, sight! For I ne'er saw true beauty till this night.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love before learning they are from enemy families.

speaker: TYBALT
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love before learning they are from enemy families.] [Keywords: meeting, love, feud] TYBALT. This, by his voice, should be a Montague. Fetch me my rapier, boy. What, dares the slave Come hither, cover'd with an antic face, To fleer and scorn at our solemnity? Now, by the stock and honour of my kin, To strike him dead I hold it not a sin.

Now old desire doth in his deathbed lie, And young affection gapes to be his heir; That fair for which love groan'd for and would die, With tender Juliet match'd, is now not fair. Now Romeo is belov'd, and loves again, Alike bewitched by the charm of looks; But to his foe suppos'd he must complain, And she steal love's sweet bait from fearful hooks. Cry but 'Ah me!' pronounce but 'love' and 'dove'; Speak to my gossip Venus one fair word, One nickname for her purblind son and heir, Young auburn Cupid, he that shot so trim When King Cophetua lov'd the beggar maid! He heareth not, he stirreth not, be moveth not; The ape is dead, and I must conjure him. I conjure thee by Rosaline's bright eyes. By her high forehead and her scarlet lip, By her fine foot, straight leg, and quivering thigh, And the demesnes that there adjacent lie, That in thy likeness thou appear to us!

That which we call a rose By any other name would smell as sweet. So Romeo would, were he not Romeo call'd, Retain that dear perfection which he owes Without that title. Romeo, doff thy name; And for that name, which is no part of thee, Take all myself.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] JULIET. Thou knowest the mask of night is on my face; Else would a maiden blush bepaint my cheek For that which thou hast heard me speak to-night. Fain would I dwell on form- fain, fain deny What I have spoke; but farewell compliment! Dost thou love me, I know thou wilt say 'Ay'; And I will take thy word. Yet, if thou swear'st, Thou mayst prove false. At lovers' perjuries, They say Jove laughs. O gentle Romeo, If thou dost love, pronounce it faithfully. Or if thou thinkest I am too quickly won, I'll frown, and be perverse, and say thee nay, So thou wilt woo; but else, not for the world. In truth, fair Montague, I am too fond, And therefore thou mayst think my haviour light; But trust me, gentleman, I'll prove more true Than those that have more cunning to be strange. I should have been more strange, I must confess, But that thou overheard'st, ere I was ware, My true-love passion. Therefore pardon me, And not impute this yielding to light love, Which the dark night hath so discovered.

text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] JULIET. Well, do not swear.
