text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] ROMEO. O, wilt thou leave me so unsatisfied?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] JULIET. What satisfaction canst thou have to-night?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] ROMEO. Th' exchange of thy love's faithful vow for mine.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] JULIET. I gave thee mine before thou didst request it; And yet I would it were to give again.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] ROMEO. Would'st thou withdraw it? For what purpose, love?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] JULIET. But to be frank and give it thee again. And yet I wish but for the thing I have. My bounty is as boundless as the sea, My love as deep; the more I give to thee, The more I have, for both are infinite. I hear some noise within. Dear love, adieu! calls within. Anon, good nurse! Sweet Montague, be true. Stay but a little, I will come again.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] ROMEO. O blessed, blessed night! I am afeard, Being in night, all this is but a dream, Too flattering-sweet to be substantial.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] JULIET. Three words, dear Romeo, and good night indeed. If that thy bent of love be honourable, Thy purpose marriage, send me word to-morrow, By one that I'll procure to come to thee, Where and what time thou wilt perform the rite; And all my fortunes at thy foot I'll lay And follow thee my lord throughout the world.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] NURSE. (within) Madam!
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] JULIET. I come, anon.- But if thou meanest not well, I do beseech thee-
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] NURSE. (within) Madam!
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] JULIET. By-and-by I come.- To cease thy suit and leave me to my grief. To-morrow will I send.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] ROMEO. So thrive my soul-
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] JULIET. A thousand times good night! Exit.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] ROMEO. A thousand times the worse, to want thy light! Love goes toward love as schoolboys from their books; But love from love, towards school with heavy looks.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] JULIET. Hist! Romeo, hist! O for a falconer's voice To lure this tassel-gentle back again! Bondage is hoarse and may not speak aloud; Else would I tear the cave where Echo lies, And make her airy tongue more hoarse than mine With repetition of my Romeo's name. Romeo!
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] ROMEO. It is my soul that calls upon my name. How silver-sweet sound lovers' tongues by night, Like softest music to attending ears!
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] JULIET. At what o'clock to-morrow Shall I send to thee?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] ROMEO. By the hour of nine.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] JULIET. I will not fail. 'Tis twenty years till then. I have forgot why I did call thee back.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] ROMEO. Let me stand here till thou remember it.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] JULIET. I shall forget, to have thee still stand there, Rememb'ring how I love thy company.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] ROMEO. And I'll still stay, to have thee still forget, Forgetting any other home but this.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] JULIET. 'Tis almost morning. I would have thee gone- And yet no farther than a wanton's bird, That lets it hop a little from her hand, Like a poor prisoner in his twisted gyves, And with a silk thread plucks it back again, So loving-jealous of his liberty.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] ROMEO. I would I were thy bird.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] JULIET. Sweet, so would I. Yet I should kill thee with much cherishing. Good night, good night! Parting is such sweet sorrow, That I shall say good night till it be morrow.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.] [Keywords: balcony, marriage] ROMEO. Sleep dwell upon thine eyes, peace in thy breast! Would I were sleep and peace, so sweet to rest! Hence will I to my ghostly father's cell, His help to crave and my dear hap to tell.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet confess love and plan marriage in the balcony scene.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] FRIAR LAURENCE. The grey-ey'd morn smiles on the frowning night, Check'ring the Eastern clouds with streaks of light; And flecked darkness like a drunkard reels From forth day's path and Titan's fiery wheels. Non, ere the sun advance his burning eye The day to cheer and night's dank dew to dry, I must up-fill this osier cage of ours With baleful weeds and precious-juiced flowers. The earth that's nature's mother is her tomb. What is her burying gave, that is her womb; And from her womb children of divers kind We sucking on her natural bosom find; Many for many virtues excellent, None but for some, and yet all different. O, mickle is the powerful grace that lies In plants, herbs, stones, and their true qualities; For naught so vile that on the earth doth live But to the earth some special good doth give; Nor aught so good but, strain'd from that fair use, Revolts from true birth, stumbling on abuse. Virtue itself turns vice, being misapplied, And vice sometime's by action dignified. Within the infant rind of this small flower Poison hath residence, and medicine power; For this, being smelt, with that part cheers each part; Being tasted, slays all senses with the heart. Two such opposed kings encamp them still In man as well as herbs- grace and rude will; And where the worser is predominant, Full soon the canker death eats up that plant.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] ROMEO. Good morrow, father.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] FRIAR LAURENCE. Benedicite! What early tongue so sweet saluteth me? Young son, it argues a distempered head So soon to bid good morrow to thy bed. Care keeps his watch in every old man's eye, And where care lodges sleep will never lie; But where unbruised youth with unstuff'd brain Doth couch his limbs, there golden sleep doth reign. Therefore thy earliness doth me assure Thou art uprous'd with some distemp'rature; Or if not so, then here I hit it right- Our Romeo hath not been in bed to-night.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] ROMEO. That last is true-the sweeter rest was mine.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] FRIAR LAURENCE. God pardon sin! Wast thou with Rosaline?
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] ROMEO. With Rosaline, my ghostly father? No. I have forgot that name, and that name's woe.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] FRIAR LAURENCE. That's my good son! But where hast thou been then?
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] ROMEO. I'll tell thee ere thou ask it me again. I have been feasting with mine enemy, Where on a sudden one hath wounded me That's by me wounded. Both our remedies Within thy help and holy physic lies. I bear no hatred, blessed man, for, lo, My intercession likewise steads my foe.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] FRIAR LAURENCE. Be plain, good son, and homely in thy drift Riddling confession finds but riddling shrift.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] ROMEO. Then plainly know my heart's dear love is set On the fair daughter of rich Capulet; As mine on hers, so hers is set on mine, And all combin'd, save what thou must combine By holy marriage. When, and where, and how We met, we woo'd, and made exchange of vow, I'll tell thee as we pass; but this I pray, That thou consent to marry us to-day.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] FRIAR LAURENCE. Holy Saint Francis! What a change is here! Is Rosaline, that thou didst love so dear, So soon forsaken? Young men's love then lies Not truly in their hearts, but in their eyes. Jesu Maria! What a deal of brine Hath wash'd thy sallow cheeks for Rosaline! How much salt water thrown away in waste, To season love, that of it doth not taste! The sun not yet thy sighs from heaven clears, Thy old groans ring yet in mine ancient ears. Lo, here upon thy cheek the stain doth sit Of an old tear that is not wash'd off yet. If e'er thou wast thyself, and these woes thine, Thou and these woes were all for Rosaline. And art thou chang'd? Pronounce this sentence then: Women may fall when there's no strength in men.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] ROMEO. Thou chid'st me oft for loving Rosaline.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] FRIAR LAURENCE. For doting, not for loving, pupil mine.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] ROMEO. And bad'st me bury love.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] FRIAR LAURENCE. Not in a grave To lay one in, another out to have.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] ROMEO. I pray thee chide not. She whom I love now Doth grace for grace and love for love allow. The other did not so.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] FRIAR LAURENCE. O, she knew well Thy love did read by rote, that could not spell. But come, young waverer, come go with me. In one respect I'll thy assistant be; For this alliance may so happy prove To turn your households' rancour to pure love.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] ROMEO. O, let us hence! I stand on sudden haste.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.] [Keywords: Friar Laurence, marriage] FRIAR LAURENCE. Wisely, and slow. They stumble that run fast.
scene_summary: Friar Laurence agrees to marry the lovers, hoping to end the feud.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. Where the devil should this Romeo be? Came he not home to-night?
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] BENVOLIO. Not to his father's. I spoke with his man.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. Why, that same pale hard-hearted wench, that Rosaline, Torments him so that he will sure run mad.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] BENVOLIO. Tybalt, the kinsman to old Capulet, Hath sent a letter to his father's house.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. A challenge, on my life.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] BENVOLIO. Romeo will answer it.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. Any man that can write may answer a letter.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] BENVOLIO. Nay, he will answer the letter's master, how he dares, being dared.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. Alas, poor Romeo, he is already dead! stabb'd with a white wench's black eye; shot through the ear with a love song; the very pin of his heart cleft with the blind bow-boy's butt-shaft; and is he a man to encounter Tybalt?
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] BENVOLIO. Why, what is Tybalt?
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. More than Prince of Cats, I can tell you. O, he's the courageous captain of compliments. He fights as you sing pricksong-keeps time, distance, and proportion; rests me his minim rest, one, two, and the third in your bosom! the very butcher of a silk button, a duellist, a duellist! a gentleman of the very first house, of the first and second cause. Ah, the immortal passado! the punto reverse! the hay.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. The pox of such antic, lisping, affecting fantasticoes- these new tuners of accent! 'By Jesu, a very good blade! a very tall man! a very good whore!' Why, is not this a lamentable thing, grandsir, that we should be thus afflicted with these strange flies, these fashion-mongers, these pardona-mi's, who stand so much on the new form that they cannot sit at ease on the old bench? O, their bones, their bones!
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] BENVOLIO. Here comes Romeo! here comes Romeo!
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. Without his roe, like a dried herring. O flesh, flesh, how art thou fishified! Now is he for the numbers that Petrarch flowed in. Laura, to his lady, was but a kitchen wench (marry, she had a better love to berhyme her), Dido a dowdy, Cleopatra a gypsy, Helen and Hero hildings and harlots, This be a gray eye or so, but not to the purpose. Signior Romeo, bon jour! There's a French salutation to your French slop. You gave us the counterfeit fairly last night.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. Good morrow to you both. What counterfeit did I give you?
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. The slip, sir, the slip. Can you not conceive?
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. Pardon, good Mercutio. My business was great, and in such a case as mine a man may strain courtesy.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. That's as much as to say, such a case as yours constrains a man to bow in the hams.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. Meaning, to cursy.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. Thou hast most kindly hit it.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. A most courteous exposition.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. Nay, I am the very pink of courtesy.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. Pink for flower.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. Why, then is my pump well-flower'd.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. Well said! Follow me this jest now till thou hast worn out thy pump, that, when the single sole of it is worn, the jest may remain, after the wearing, solely singular.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. O single-sold jest, solely singular for the singleness!
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. Come between us, good Benvolio! My wits faint.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. Swits and spurs, swits and spurs! or I'll cry a match.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. Nay, if our wits run the wild-goose chase, I am done; for thou hast more of the wild goose in one of thy wits than, I am sure, I have in my whole five. Was I with you there for the goose?
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. Thou wast never with me for anything when thou wast not there for the goose.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. I will bite thee by the ear for that jest.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. Nay, good goose, bite not!
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. Thy wit is a very bitter sweeting; it is a most sharp sauce.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. And is it not, then, well serv'd in to a sweet goose?
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. O, here's a wit of cheveril, that stretches from an inch narrow to an ell broad!
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. I stretch it out for that word 'broad,' which, added to the goose, proves thee far and wide a broad goose.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. Why, is not this better now than groaning for love? Now art thou sociable, now art thou Romeo; now art thou what thou art, by art as well as by nature. For this drivelling love is like a great natural that runs lolling up and down to hide his bauble in a hole.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] BENVOLIO. Stop there, stop there!
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. Thou desirest me to stop in my tale against the hair.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] BENVOLIO. Thou wouldst else have made thy tale large.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. O, thou art deceiv'd! I would have made it short; for I was come to the whole depth of my tale, and meant indeed to occupy the argument no longer.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. Here's goodly gear!
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. A sail, a sail!
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] BENVOLIO. Two, two! a shirt and a smock.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. My fan, Peter.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. Good Peter, to hide her face; for her fan's the fairer face of the two.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. God ye good morrow, gentlemen.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. God ye good-den, fair gentlewoman.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. Is it good-den?
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. 'Tis no less, I tell ye; for the bawdy hand of the dial is now upon the prick of noon.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. Out upon you! What a man are you!
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. One, gentlewoman, that God hath made for himself to mar.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. By my troth, it is well said. 'For himself to mar,' quoth 'a? Gentlemen, can any of you tell me where I may find the young Romeo?
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. I can tell you; but young Romeo will be older when you have found him than he was when you sought him. I am the youngest of that name, for fault of a worse.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. You say well.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. Yea, is the worst well? Very well took, i' faith! wisely, wisely.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. If you be he, sir, I desire some confidence with you.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] BENVOLIO. She will endite him to some supper.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. A bawd, a bawd, a bawd! So ho!
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. What hast thou found?
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. No hare, sir; unless a hare, sir, in a lenten pie, that is something stale and hoar ere it be spent He walks by them and sings. An old hare hoar, And an old hare hoar, Is very good meat in Lent; But a hare that is hoar Is too much for a score When it hoars ere it be spent. Romeo, will you come to your father's? We'll to dinner thither.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. I will follow you.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] MERCUTIO. Farewell, ancient lady. Farewell, lady, lady, lady.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. Marry, farewell! I Pray you, Sir, what saucy merchant was this that was so full of his ropery?
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. A gentleman, nurse, that loves to hear himself talk and will speak more in a minute than he will stand to in a month.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. An 'a speak anything against me, I'll take him down, an 'a were lustier than he is, and twenty such jacks; and if I cannot, I'll find those that shall. Scurvy knave! I am none of his flirt-gills; I am none of his skains-mates. And thou must stand by too, and suffer every knave to use me at his pleasure!
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: PETER
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] PETER. I saw no man use you at his pleasure. If I had, my weapon should quickly have been out, I warrant you. I dare draw as soon as another man, if I see occasion in a good quarrel, and the law on my side.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. Now, afore God, I am so vexed that every part about me quivers. Scurvy knave! Pray you, sir, a word; and, as I told you, my young lady bid me enquire you out. What she bid me say, I will keep to myself; but first let me tell ye, if ye should lead her into a fool's paradise, as they say, it were a very gross kind of behaviour, as they say; for the gentlewoman is young; and therefore, if you should deal double with her, truly it were an ill thing to be off'red to any gentlewoman, and very weak dealing.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. Nurse, commend me to thy lady and mistress. I protest unto thee-
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. Good heart, and i' faith I will tell her as much. Lord, Lord! she will be a joyful woman.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. What wilt thou tell her, nurse? Thou dost not mark me.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. I will tell her, sir, that you do protest, which, as I take it, is a gentlemanlike offer.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. Bid her devise Some means to come to shrift this afternoon; And there she shall at Friar Laurence' cell Be shriv'd and married. Here is for thy pains.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. No, truly, sir; not a penny.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. Go to! I say you shall.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. This afternoon, sir? Well, she shall be there.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. And stay, good nurse, behind the abbey wall. Within this hour my man shall be with thee And bring thee cords made like a tackled stair, Which to the high topgallant of my joy Must be my convoy in the secret night.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: FAREWELL
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] FAREWELL. Be trusty, and I'll quit thy pains.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: FAREWELL
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] FAREWELL. Commend me to thy mistress.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. Now God in heaven bless thee! Hark you, sir.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. What say'st thou, my dear nurse?
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. Is your man secret? Did you ne'er hear say, Two may keep counsel, putting one away?
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. I warrant thee my man's as true as steel.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. Well, sir, my mistress is the sweetest lady. Lord, Lord! when 'twas a little prating thing- O, there is a nobleman in town, one Paris, that would fain lay knife aboard; but she, good soul, had as lieve see a toad, a very toad, as see him. I anger her sometimes, and tell her that Paris is the properer man; but I'll warrant you, when I say so, she looks as pale as any clout in the versal world. Doth not rosemary and Romeo begin both with a letter?
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. Ay, nurse; what of that? Both with an R.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. Ah, mocker! that's the dog's name. R is for the- No; I know it begins with some other letter; and she hath the prettiest sententious of it, of you and rosemary, that it would do you good to hear it.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] ROMEO. Commend me to thy lady.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. Ay, a thousand times. Peter!
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.] [Keywords: Nurse, message] NURSE. Peter, take my fan, and go before, and apace.
scene_summary: Romeo arranges marriage plans through the Nurse.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] JULIET. The clock struck nine when I did send the nurse; In half an hour she promis'd to return. Perchance she cannot meet him. That's not so. O, she is lame! Love's heralds should be thoughts, Which ten times faster glide than the sun's beams Driving back shadows over low'ring hills. Therefore do nimble-pinion'd doves draw Love, And therefore hath the wind-swift Cupid wings. Now is the sun upon the highmost hill Of this day's journey, and from nine till twelve Is three long hours; yet she is not come. Had she affections and warm youthful blood, She would be as swift in motion as a ball; My words would bandy her to my sweet love, And his to me, But old folks, many feign as they were dead- Unwieldy, slow, heavy and pale as lead.
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] NURSE. Peter, stay at the gate.
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] JULIET. Now, good sweet nurse- O Lord, why look'st thou sad? Though news be sad, yet tell them merrily; If good, thou shamest the music of sweet news By playing it to me with so sour a face.
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] NURSE. I am aweary, give me leave awhile. Fie, how my bones ache! What a jaunce have I had!
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] JULIET. I would thou hadst my bones, and I thy news. Nay, come, I pray thee speak. Good, good nurse, speak.
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] NURSE. Jesu, what haste! Can you not stay awhile? Do you not see that I am out of breath?
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] JULIET. How art thou out of breath when thou hast breath To say to me that thou art out of breath? The excuse that thou dost make in this delay Is longer than the tale thou dost excuse. Is thy news good or bad? Answer to that. Say either, and I'll stay the circumstance. Let me be satisfied, is't good or bad?
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] NURSE. Well, you have made a simple choice; you know not how to choose a man. Romeo? No, not he. Though his face be better than any man's, yet his leg excels all men's; and for a hand and a foot, and a body, though they be not to be talk'd on, yet they are past compare. He is not the flower of courtesy, but, I'll warrant him, as gentle as a lamb. Go thy ways, wench; serve God. What, have you din'd at home?
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] JULIET. No, no. But all this did I know before. What says he of our marriage? What of that?
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] NURSE. Lord, how my head aches! What a head have I! It beats as it would fall in twenty pieces. My back o' t' other side,- ah, my back, my back! Beshrew your heart for sending me about To catch my death with jauncing up and down!
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] JULIET. I' faith, I am sorry that thou art not well. Sweet, sweet, sweet nurse, tell me, what says my love?
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] NURSE. Your love says, like an honest gentleman, and a courteous, and a kind, and a handsome; and, I warrant, a virtuous- Where is your mother?
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] JULIET. Where is my mother? Why, she is within. Where should she be? How oddly thou repliest! 'Your love says, like an honest gentleman, "Where is your mother?"'
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] NURSE. O God's Lady dear! Are you so hot? Marry come up, I trow. Is this the poultice for my aching bones? Henceforward do your messages yourself.
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] JULIET. Here's such a coil! Come, what says Romeo?
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] NURSE. Have you got leave to go to shrift to-day?
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] NURSE. Then hie you hence to Friar Laurence' cell; There stays a husband to make you a wife. Now comes the wanton blood up in your cheeks: They'll be in scarlet straight at any news. Hie you to church; I must another way, To fetch a ladder, by the which your love Must climb a bird's nest soon when it is dark. I am the drudge, and toil in your delight; But you shall bear the burthen soon at night. Go; I'll to dinner; hie you to the cell.
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: Nurse, Juliet] JULIET. Hie to high fortune! Honest nurse, farewell.
scene_summary: The Nurse tells Juliet about Romeo’s marriage plan.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.] [Keywords: secret marriage] FRIAR LAURENCE. So smile the heavens upon this holy act That after-hours with sorrow chide us not!
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.] [Keywords: secret marriage] ROMEO. Amen, amen! But come what sorrow can, It cannot countervail the exchange of joy That one short minute gives me in her sight. Do thou but close our hands with holy words, Then love-devouring death do what he dare- It is enough I may but call her mine.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.] [Keywords: secret marriage] FRIAR LAURENCE. These violent delights have violent ends And in their triumph die, like fire and powder, Which, as they kiss, consume. The sweetest honey Is loathsome in his own deliciousness And in the taste confounds the appetite. Therefore love moderately: long love doth so; Too swift arrives as tardy as too slow.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.] [Keywords: secret marriage] JULIET. Good even to my ghostly confessor.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.] [Keywords: secret marriage] FRIAR LAURENCE. Romeo shall thank thee, daughter, for us both.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.] [Keywords: secret marriage] JULIET. As much to him, else is his thanks too much.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.] [Keywords: secret marriage] ROMEO. Ah, Juliet, if the measure of thy joy Be heap'd like mine, and that thy skill be more To blazon it, then sweeten with thy breath This neighbour air, and let rich music's tongue Unfold the imagin'd happiness that both Receive in either by this dear encounter.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.] [Keywords: secret marriage] JULIET. Conceit, more rich in matter than in words, Brags of his substance, not of ornament. They are but beggars that can count their worth; But my true love is grown to such excess cannot sum up sum of half my wealth.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.] [Keywords: secret marriage] FRIAR LAURENCE. Come, come with me, and we will make short work; For, by your leaves, you shall not stay alone Till Holy Church incorporate two in one.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet marry secretly.
