speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] BENVOLIO. I pray thee, good Mercutio, let's retire. The day is hot, the Capulets abroad. And if we meet, we shall not scape a brawl, For now, these hot days, is the mad blood stirring.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. Thou art like one of these fellows that, when he enters the confines of a tavern, claps me his sword upon the table and says 'God send me no need of thee!' and by the operation of the second cup draws him on the drawer, when indeed there is no need.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] BENVOLIO. Am I like such a fellow?
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. Come, come, thou art as hot a jack in thy mood as any in Italy; and as soon moved to be moody, and as soon moody to be moved.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] BENVOLIO. And what to?
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. Nay, an there were two such, we should have none shortly, for one would kill the other. Thou! why, thou wilt quarrel with a man that hath a hair more or a hair less in his beard than thou hast. Thou wilt quarrel with a man for cracking nuts, having no other reason but because thou hast hazel eyes. What eye but such an eye would spy out such a quarrel? Thy head is as full of quarrels as an egg is full of meat; and yet thy head hath been beaten as addle as an egg for quarrelling. Thou hast quarrell'd with a man for coughing in the street, because he hath wakened thy dog that hath lain asleep in the sun. Didst thou not fall out with a tailor for wearing his new doublet before Easter, with another for tying his new shoes with an old riband? And yet thou wilt tutor me from quarrelling!
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] BENVOLIO. An I were so apt to quarrel as thou art, any man should buy the fee simple of my life for an hour and a quarter.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. The fee simple? O simple!
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] BENVOLIO. By my head, here come the Capulets.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. By my heel, I care not.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: TYBALT
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] TYBALT. Follow me close, for I will speak to them. Gentlemen, good den. A word with one of you.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. And but one word with one of us? Couple it with something; make it a word and a blow.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: TYBALT
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] TYBALT. You shall find me apt enough to that, sir, an you will give me occasion.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. Could you not take some occasion without giving?
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: TYBALT
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] TYBALT. Mercutio, thou consortest with Romeo.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. Consort? What, dost thou make us minstrels? An thou make minstrels of us, look to hear nothing but discords. Here's my fiddlestick; here's that shall make you dance. Zounds, consort!
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] BENVOLIO. We talk here in the public haunt of men. Either withdraw unto some private place And reason coldly of your grievances, Or else depart. Here all eyes gaze on us.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. Men's eyes were made to look, and let them gaze. I will not budge for no man's pleasure,
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: TYBALT
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] TYBALT. Well, peace be with you, sir. Here comes my man.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. But I'll be hang'd, sir, if he wear your livery. Marry, go before to field, he'll be your follower! Your worship in that sense may call him man.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: TYBALT
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] TYBALT. Romeo, the love I bear thee can afford No better term than this: thou art a villain.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] ROMEO. Tybalt, the reason that I have to love thee Doth much excuse the appertaining rage To such a greeting. Villain am I none. Therefore farewell. I see thou knowest me not.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: TYBALT
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] TYBALT. Boy, this shall not excuse the injuries That thou hast done me; therefore turn and draw.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] ROMEO. I do protest I never injur'd thee, But love thee better than thou canst devise Till thou shalt know the reason of my love; And so good Capulet, which name I tender As dearly as mine own, be satisfied.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. O calm, dishonourable, vile submission! Alla stoccata carries it away. Tybalt, you ratcatcher, will you walk?
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: TYBALT
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] TYBALT. What wouldst thou have with me?
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. Good King of Cats, nothing but one of your nine lives. That I mean to make bold withal, and, as you shall use me hereafter, dry-beat the rest of the eight. Will you pluck your sword out of his pitcher by the ears? Make haste, lest mine be about your ears ere it be out.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: TYBALT
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] TYBALT. I am for you.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] ROMEO. Gentle Mercutio, put thy rapier up.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. Come, sir, your passado!
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] ROMEO. Draw, Benvolio; beat down their weapons. Gentlemen, for shame! forbear this outrage! Tybalt, Mercutio, the Prince expressly hath Forbid this bandying in Verona streets. Hold, Tybalt! Good Mercutio! Tybalt under Romeo's arm thrusts Mercutio in, and flies .
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. I am hurt. A plague o' both your houses! I am sped. Is he gone and hath nothing?
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] BENVOLIO. What, art thou hurt?
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. Ay, ay, a scratch, a scratch. Marry, 'tis enough. Where is my page? Go, villain, fetch a surgeon.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] ROMEO. Courage, man. The hurt cannot be much.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. No, 'tis not so deep as a well, nor so wide as a church door; but 'tis enough, 'twill serve. Ask for me to-morrow, and you shall find me a grave man. I am peppered, I warrant, for this world. A plague o' both your houses! Zounds, a dog, a rat, a mouse, a cat, to scratch a man to death! a braggart, a rogue, a villain, that fights by the book of arithmetic! Why the devil came you between us? I was hurt under your arm.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] ROMEO. I thought all for the best.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MERCUTIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MERCUTIO. Help me into some house, Benvolio, Or I shall faint. A plague o' both your houses! They have made worms' meat of me. I have it, And soundly too. Your houses!
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] ROMEO. This gentleman, the Prince's near ally, My very friend, hath got this mortal hurt In my behalf- my reputation stain'd With Tybalt's slander- Tybalt, that an hour Hath been my kinsman. O sweet Juliet, Thy beauty hath made me effeminate And in my temper soft'ned valour's steel.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] BENVOLIO. O Romeo, Romeo, brave Mercutio's dead! That gallant spirit hath aspir'd the clouds, Which too untimely here did scorn the earth.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] ROMEO. This day's black fate on moe days doth depend; This but begins the woe others must end.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] BENVOLIO. Here comes the furious Tybalt back again.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] ROMEO. Alive in triumph, and Mercutio slain? Away to heaven respective lenity, And fire-ey'd fury be my conduct now! Now, Tybalt, take the 'villain' back again That late thou gavest me; for Mercutio's soul Is but a little way above our heads, Staying for thine to keep him company. Either thou or I, or both, must go with him.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: TYBALT
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] TYBALT. Thou, wretched boy, that didst consort him here, Shalt with him hence.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] ROMEO. This shall determine that. They fight. Tybalt falls.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] BENVOLIO. Romeo, away, be gone! The citizens are up, and Tybalt slain. Stand not amaz'd. The Prince will doom thee death If thou art taken. Hence, be gone, away!
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] ROMEO. O, I am fortune's fool!
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] BENVOLIO. Why dost thou stay?
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] CITIZEN. Which way ran he that kill'd Mercutio? Tybalt, that murtherer, which way ran he?
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] BENVOLIO. There lies that Tybalt.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: CITIZEN
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] CITIZEN. Up, sir, go with me. I charge thee in the Prince's name obey.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: PRINCE
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] PRINCE. Where are the vile beginners of this fray?
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] BENVOLIO. O noble Prince. I can discover all The unlucky manage of this fatal brawl. There lies the man, slain by young Romeo, That slew thy kinsman, brave Mercutio.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] CAPULET. Wife. Tybalt, my cousin! O my brother's child! O Prince! O husband! O, the blood is spill'd Of my dear kinsman! Prince, as thou art true, For blood of ours shed blood of Montague. O cousin, cousin!
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: PRINCE
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] PRINCE. Benvolio, who began this bloody fray?
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: BENVOLIO
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] BENVOLIO. Tybalt, here slain, whom Romeo's hand did stay. Romeo, that spoke him fair, bid him bethink How nice the quarrel was, and urg'd withal Your high displeasure. All this- uttered With gentle breath, calm look, knees humbly bow'd- Could not take truce with the unruly spleen Of Tybalt deaf to peace, but that he tilts With piercing steel at bold Mercutio's breast; Who, all as hot, turns deadly point to point, And, with a martial scorn, with one hand beats Cold death aside and with the other sends It back to Tybalt, whose dexterity Retorts it. Romeo he cries aloud, 'Hold, friends! friends, part!' and swifter than his tongue, His agile arm beats down their fatal points, And 'twixt them rushes; underneath whose arm An envious thrust from Tybalt hit the life Of stout Mercutio, and then Tybalt fled; But by-and-by comes back to Romeo, Who had but newly entertain'd revenge, And to't they go like lightning; for, ere I Could draw to part them, was stout Tybalt slain; And, as he fell, did Romeo turn and fly. This is the truth, or let Benvolio die.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] CAPULET. Wife. He is a kinsman to the Montague; Affection makes him false, he speaks not true. Some twenty of them fought in this black strife, And all those twenty could but kill one life. I beg for justice, which thou, Prince, must give. Romeo slew Tybalt; Romeo must not live.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: PRINCE
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] PRINCE. Romeo slew him; he slew Mercutio. Who now the price of his dear blood doth owe?
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: MONTAGUE
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] MONTAGUE. Not Romeo, Prince; he was Mercutio's friend; His fault concludes but what the law should end, The life of Tybalt.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: PRINCE
text: [Scene: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.] [Keywords: Tybalt, Mercutio, banishment] PRINCE. And for that offence Immediately we do exile him hence. I have an interest in your hate's proceeding, My blood for your rude brawls doth lie a-bleeding; But I'll amerce you with so strong a fine That you shall all repent the loss of mine. I will be deaf to pleading and excuses; Nor tears nor prayers shall purchase out abuses. Therefore use none. Let Romeo hence in haste, Else, when he is found, that hour is his last. Bear hence this body, and attend our will. Mercy but murders, pardoning those that kill.
scene_summary: Tybalt kills Mercutio; Romeo kills Tybalt and is banished.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] JULIET. Gallop apace, you fiery-footed steeds, Towards Phoebus' lodging! Such a wagoner As Phaeton would whip you to the West And bring in cloudy night immediately. Spread thy close curtain, love-performing night, That runaway eyes may wink, and Romeo Leap to these arms untalk'd of and unseen. Lovers can see to do their amorous rites By their own beauties; or, if love be blind, It best agrees with night. Come, civil night, Thou sober-suited matron, all in black, And learn me how to lose a winning match, Play'd for a pair of stainless maidenhoods. Hood my unmann'd blood, bating in my cheeks, With thy black mantle till strange love, grown bold, Think true love acted simple modesty. Come, night; come, Romeo; come, thou day in night; For thou wilt lie upon the wings of night Whiter than new snow upon a raven's back. Come, gentle night; come, loving, black-brow'd night; Give me my Romeo; and, when he shall die, Take him and cut him out in little stars, And he will make the face of heaven so fine That all the world will be in love with night And pay no worship to the garish sun. O, I have bought the mansion of a love, But not possess'd it; and though I am sold, Not yet enjoy'd. So tedious is this day As is the night before some festival To an impatient child that hath new robes And may not wear them. O, here comes my nurse,
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] NURSE. Ay, ay, the cords.
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] JULIET. Ah me! what news? Why dost thou wring thy hands?
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] NURSE. Ah, weraday! he's dead, he's dead, he's dead! We are undone, lady, we are undone! Alack the day! he's gone, he's kill'd, he's dead!
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] JULIET. Can heaven be so envious?
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] NURSE. Romeo can, Though heaven cannot. O Romeo, Romeo! Who ever would have thought it? Romeo!
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] JULIET. What devil art thou that dost torment me thus? This torture should be roar'd in dismal hell. Hath Romeo slain himself? Say thou but 'I,' And that bare vowel 'I' shall poison more Than the death-darting eye of cockatrice. I am not I, if there be such an 'I'; Or those eyes shut that make thee answer 'I.' If he be slain, say 'I'; or if not, 'no.' Brief sounds determine of my weal or woe.
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] NURSE. I saw the wound, I saw it with mine eyes, (God save the mark!) here on his manly breast. A piteous corse, a bloody piteous corse; Pale, pale as ashes, all bedaub'd in blood, All in gore-blood. I swounded at the sight.
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] JULIET. O, break, my heart! poor bankrout, break at once! To prison, eyes; ne'er look on liberty! Vile earth, to earth resign; end motion here, And thou and Romeo press one heavy bier!
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] NURSE. O Tybalt, Tybalt, the best friend I had! O courteous Tybalt! honest gentleman That ever I should live to see thee dead!
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] JULIET. What storm is this that blows so contrary? Is Romeo slaught'red, and is Tybalt dead? My dear-lov'd cousin, and my dearer lord? Then, dreadful trumpet, sound the general doom! For who is living, if those two are gone?
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] NURSE. Tybalt is gone, and Romeo banished; Romeo that kill'd him, he is banished.
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] JULIET. O God! Did Romeo's hand shed Tybalt's blood?
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] NURSE. It did, it did! alas the day, it did!
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] JULIET. O serpent heart, hid with a flow'ring face! Did ever dragon keep so fair a cave? Beautiful tyrant! fiend angelical! Dove-feather'd raven! wolvish-ravening lamb! Despised substance of divinest show! Just opposite to what thou justly seem'st- A damned saint, an honourable villain! O nature, what hadst thou to do in hell When thou didst bower the spirit of a fiend In mortal paradise of such sweet flesh? Was ever book containing such vile matter So fairly bound? O, that deceit should dwell In such a gorgeous palace!
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] NURSE. There's no trust, No faith, no honesty in men; all perjur'd, All forsworn, all naught, all dissemblers. Ah, where's my man? Give me some aqua vitae. These griefs, these woes, these sorrows make me old. Shame come to Romeo!
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] JULIET. Blister'd be thy tongue For such a wish! He was not born to shame. Upon his brow shame is asham'd to sit; For 'tis a throne where honour may be crown'd Sole monarch of the universal earth. O, what a beast was I to chide at him!
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] NURSE. Will you speak well of him that kill'd your cousin?
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] JULIET. Shall I speak ill of him that is my husband? Ah, poor my lord, what tongue shall smooth thy name When I, thy three-hours wife, have mangled it? But wherefore, villain, didst thou kill my cousin? That villain cousin would have kill'd my husband. Back, foolish tears, back to your native spring! Your tributary drops belong to woe, Which you, mistaking, offer up to joy. My husband lives, that Tybalt would have slain; And Tybalt's dead, that would have slain my husband. All this is comfort; wherefore weep I then? Some word there was, worser than Tybalt's death, That murd'red me. I would forget it fain; But O, it presses to my memory Like damned guilty deeds to sinners' minds! 'Tybalt is dead, and Romeo- banished.' That 'banished,' that one word 'banished,' Hath slain ten thousand Tybalts. Tybalt's death Was woe enough, if it had ended there; Or, if sour woe delights in fellowship And needly will be rank'd with other griefs, Why followed not, when she said 'Tybalt's dead,' Thy father, or thy mother, nay, or both, Which modern lamentation might have mov'd? But with a rearward following Tybalt's death, 'Romeo is banished'- to speak that word Is father, mother, Tybalt, Romeo, Juliet, All slain, all dead. 'Romeo is banished'- There is no end, no limit, measure, bound, In that word's death; no words can that woe sound. Where is my father and my mother, nurse?
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] NURSE. Weeping and wailing over Tybalt's corse. Will you go to them? I will bring you thither.
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] JULIET. Wash they his wounds with tears? Mine shall be spent, When theirs are dry, for Romeo's banishment. Take up those cords. Poor ropes, you are beguil'd, Both you and I, for Romeo is exil'd. He made you for a highway to my bed; But I, a maid, die maiden-widowed. Come, cords; come, nurse. I'll to my wedding bed; And death, not Romeo, take my maidenhead!
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] NURSE. Hie to your chamber. I'll find Romeo To comfort you. I wot well where he is. Hark ye, your Romeo will be here at night. I'll to him; he is hid at Laurence' cell.
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.] [Keywords: Juliet, Tybalt, conflict] JULIET. O, find him! give this ring to my true knight And bid him come to take his last farewell.
scene_summary: Juliet learns Romeo killed Tybalt and is torn between love and family loyalty.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] FRIAR LAURENCE. Romeo, come forth; come forth, thou fearful man. Affliction is enanmour'd of thy parts, And thou art wedded to calamity.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] ROMEO. Father, what news? What is the Prince's doom What sorrow craves acquaintance at my hand That I yet know not?
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] FRIAR LAURENCE. Too familiar Is my dear son with such sour company. I bring thee tidings of the Prince's doom.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] ROMEO. What less than doomsday is the Prince's doom?
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] FRIAR LAURENCE. A gentler judgment vanish'd from his lips- Not body's death, but body's banishment.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] ROMEO. Ha, banishment? Be merciful, say 'death'; For exile hath more terror in his look, Much more than death. Do not say 'banishment.'
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] FRIAR LAURENCE. Hence from Verona art thou banished. Be patient, for the world is broad and wide.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] ROMEO. There is no world without Verona walls, But purgatory, torture, hell itself. Hence banished is banish'd from the world, And world's exile is death. Then 'banishment' Is death misterm'd. Calling death 'banishment,' Thou cut'st my head off with a golden axe And smilest upon the stroke that murders me.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] FRIAR LAURENCE. O deadly sin! O rude unthankfulness! Thy fault our law calls death; but the kind Prince, Taking thy part, hath brush'd aside the law, And turn'd that black word death to banishment. This is dear mercy, and thou seest it not.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] ROMEO. 'Tis torture, and not mercy. Heaven is here, Where Juliet lives; and every cat and dog And little mouse, every unworthy thing, Live here in heaven and may look on her; But Romeo may not. More validity, More honourable state, more courtship lives In carrion flies than Romeo. They may seize On the white wonder of dear Juliet's hand And steal immortal blessing from her lips, Who, even in pure and vestal modesty, Still blush, as thinking their own kisses sin; But Romeo may not- he is banished. This may flies do, when I from this must fly; They are free men, but I am banished. And sayest thou yet that exile is not death? Hadst thou no poison mix'd, no sharp-ground knife, No sudden mean of death, though ne'er so mean, But 'banished' to kill me- 'banished'? O friar, the damned use that word in hell; Howling attends it! How hast thou the heart, Being a divine, a ghostly confessor, A sin-absolver, and my friend profess'd, To mangle me with that word 'banished'?
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] FRIAR LAURENCE. Thou fond mad man, hear me a little speak.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] ROMEO. O, thou wilt speak again of banishment.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] FRIAR LAURENCE. I'll give thee armour to keep off that word; Adversity's sweet milk, philosophy, To comfort thee, though thou art banished.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] ROMEO. Yet 'banished'? Hang up philosophy! Unless philosophy can make a Juliet, Displant a town, reverse a prince's doom, It helps not, it prevails not. Talk no more.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] FRIAR LAURENCE. O, then I see that madmen have no ears.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] ROMEO. How should they, when that wise men have no eyes?
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] FRIAR LAURENCE. Let me dispute with thee of thy estate.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] ROMEO. Thou canst not speak of that thou dost not feel. Wert thou as young as I, Juliet thy love, An hour but married, Tybalt murdered, Doting like me, and like me banished, Then mightst thou speak, then mightst thou tear thy hair, And fall upon the ground, as I do now, Taking the measure of an unmade grave. Knock .
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] FRIAR LAURENCE. Arise; one knocks. Good Romeo, hide thyself.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] ROMEO. Not I; unless the breath of heartsick groans, Mist-like infold me from the search of eyes. Knock.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] FRIAR LAURENCE. Hark, how they knock! Who's there? Romeo, arise; Thou wilt be taken.- Stay awhile!- Stand up; Knock. Run to my study.- By-and-by!- God's will, What simpleness is this.- I come, I come! Knock. Who knocks so hard? Whence come you? What's your will?
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] NURSE. Let me come in, and you shall know my errand. I come from Lady Juliet.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] FRIAR LAURENCE. Welcome then.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] NURSE. O holy friar, O, tell me, holy friar, Where is my lady's lord, where's Romeo?
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] FRIAR LAURENCE. There on the ground, with his own tears made drunk.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] NURSE. O, he is even in my mistress' case, Just in her case!
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] FRIAR LAURENCE. O woeful sympathy! Piteous predicament!
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] NURSE. Even so lies she, Blubb'ring and weeping, weeping and blubbering. Stand up, stand up! Stand, an you be a man. For Juliet's sake, for her sake, rise and stand! Why should you fall into so deep an O?
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] ROMEO. (rises) Nurse-
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] NURSE. Ah sir! ah sir! Well, death's the end of all.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] ROMEO. Spakest thou of Juliet? How is it with her? Doth not she think me an old murtherer, Now I have stain'd the childhood of our joy With blood remov'd but little from her own? Where is she? and how doth she! and what says My conceal'd lady to our cancell'd love?
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] NURSE. O, she says nothing, sir, but weeps and weeps; And now falls on her bed, and then starts up, And Tybalt calls; and then on Romeo cries, And then down falls again.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] ROMEO. As if that name, Shot from the deadly level of a gun, Did murther her; as that name's cursed hand Murder'd her kinsman. O, tell me, friar, tell me, In what vile part of this anatomy Doth my name lodge? Tell me, that I may sack The hateful mansion.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] FRIAR LAURENCE. Hold thy desperate hand. Art thou a man? Thy form cries out thou art; Thy tears are womanish, thy wild acts denote The unreasonable fury of a beast. Unseemly woman in a seeming man! Or ill-beseeming beast in seeming both! Thou hast amaz'd me. By my holy order, I thought thy disposition better temper'd. Hast thou slain Tybalt? Wilt thou slay thyself? And slay thy lady that in thy life lives, By doing damned hate upon thyself? Why railest thou on thy birth, the heaven, and earth? Since birth and heaven and earth, all three do meet In thee at once; which thou at once wouldst lose. Fie, fie, thou shamest thy shape, thy love, thy wit, Which, like a usurer, abound'st in all, And usest none in that true use indeed Which should bedeck thy shape, thy love, thy wit. Thy noble shape is but a form of wax Digressing from the valour of a man; Thy dear love sworn but hollow perjury, Killing that love which thou hast vow'd to cherish; Thy wit, that ornament to shape and love, Misshapen in the conduct of them both, Like powder in a skilless soldier's flask, Is set afire by thine own ignorance, And thou dismemb'red with thine own defence. What, rouse thee, man! Thy Juliet is alive, For whose dear sake thou wast but lately dead. There art thou happy. Tybalt would kill thee, But thou slewest Tybalt. There art thou happy too. The law, that threat'ned death, becomes thy friend And turns it to exile. There art thou happy. A pack of blessings light upon thy back; Happiness courts thee in her best array; But, like a misbehav'd and sullen wench, Thou pout'st upon thy fortune and thy love. Take heed, take heed, for such die miserable. Go get thee to thy love, as was decreed, Ascend her chamber, hence and comfort her. But look thou stay not till the watch be set, For then thou canst not pass to Mantua, Where thou shalt live till we can find a time To blaze your marriage, reconcile your friends, Beg pardon of the Prince, and call thee back With twenty hundred thousand times more joy Than thou went'st forth in lamentation. Go before, nurse. Commend me to thy lady, And bid her hasten all the house to bed, Which heavy sorrow makes them apt unto. Romeo is coming.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] NURSE. O Lord, I could have stay'd here all the night To hear good counsel. O, what learning is! My lord, I'll tell my lady you will come.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] ROMEO. Do so, and bid my sweet prepare to chide.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] NURSE. Here is a ring she bid me give you, sir. Hie you, make haste, for it grows very late. Exit.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] ROMEO. How well my comfort is reviv'd by this!
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: FRIAR LAURENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] FRIAR LAURENCE. Go hence; good night; and here stands all your state: Either be gone before the watch be set, Or by the break of day disguis'd from hence. Sojourn in Mantua. I'll find out your man, And he shall signify from time to time Every good hap to you that chances here. Give me thy hand. 'Tis late. Farewell; good night.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.] [Keywords: banishment, Friar Laurence] ROMEO. But that a joy past joy calls out on me, It were a grief so brief to part with thee. Farewell.
scene_summary: Romeo despairs over banishment while Friar Laurence gives advice.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Capulet promises Paris that Juliet will marry him.] [Keywords: Capulet, Paris] CAPULET. Things have fall'n out, sir, so unluckily That we have had no time to move our daughter. Look you, she lov'd her kinsman Tybalt dearly, And so did I. Well, we were born to die. 'Tis very late; she'll not come down to-night. I promise you, but for your company, I would have been abed an hour ago.
scene_summary: Capulet promises Paris that Juliet will marry him.

speaker: PAR
text: [Scene: Capulet promises Paris that Juliet will marry him.] [Keywords: Capulet, Paris] PAR. These times of woe afford no tune to woo. Madam, good night. Commend me to your daughter.
scene_summary: Capulet promises Paris that Juliet will marry him.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Capulet promises Paris that Juliet will marry him.] [Keywords: Capulet, Paris] LADY. I will, and know her mind early to-morrow; To-night she's mew'd up to her heaviness.
scene_summary: Capulet promises Paris that Juliet will marry him.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Capulet promises Paris that Juliet will marry him.] [Keywords: Capulet, Paris] CAPULET. Sir Paris, I will make a desperate tender Of my child's love. I think she will be rul'd In all respects by me; nay more, I doubt it not. Wife, go you to her ere you go to bed; Acquaint her here of my son Paris' love And bid her (mark you me?) on Wednesday next- But, soft! what day is this?
scene_summary: Capulet promises Paris that Juliet will marry him.

speaker: PAR
text: [Scene: Capulet promises Paris that Juliet will marry him.] [Keywords: Capulet, Paris] PAR. Monday, my lord.
scene_summary: Capulet promises Paris that Juliet will marry him.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Capulet promises Paris that Juliet will marry him.] [Keywords: Capulet, Paris] CAPULET. Monday! ha, ha! Well, Wednesday is too soon. Thursday let it be- a Thursday, tell her She shall be married to this noble earl. Will you be ready? Do you like this haste? We'll keep no great ado- a friend or two; For hark you, Tybalt being slain so late, It may be thought we held him carelessly, Being our kinsman, if we revel much. Therefore we'll have some half a dozen friends, And there an end. But what say you to Thursday?
scene_summary: Capulet promises Paris that Juliet will marry him.

speaker: PAR
text: [Scene: Capulet promises Paris that Juliet will marry him.] [Keywords: Capulet, Paris] PAR. My lord, I would that Thursday were to-morrow.
scene_summary: Capulet promises Paris that Juliet will marry him.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Capulet promises Paris that Juliet will marry him.] [Keywords: Capulet, Paris] CAPULET. Well, get you gone. A Thursday be it then. Go you to Juliet ere you go to bed; Prepare her, wife, against this wedding day. Farewell, my lord.- Light to my chamber, ho! Afore me, It is so very very late That we may call it early by-and-by. Good night.
scene_summary: Capulet promises Paris that Juliet will marry him.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Wilt thou be gone? It is not yet near day. It was the nightingale, and not the lark, That pierc'd the fearful hollow of thine ear. Nightly she sings on yond pomegranate tree. Believe me, love, it was the nightingale.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] ROMEO. It was the lark, the herald of the morn; No nightingale. Look, love, what envious streaks Do lace the severing clouds in yonder East. Night's candles are burnt out, and jocund day Stands tiptoe on the misty mountain tops. I must be gone and live, or stay and die.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Yond light is not daylight; I know it, I. It is some meteor that the sun exhales To be to thee this night a torchbearer And light thee on the way to Mantua. Therefore stay yet; thou need'st not to be gone.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] ROMEO. Let me be ta'en, let me be put to death. I am content, so thou wilt have it so. I'll say yon grey is not the morning's eye, 'Tis but the pale reflex of Cynthia's brow; Nor that is not the lark whose notes do beat The vaulty heaven so high above our heads. I have more care to stay than will to go. Come, death, and welcome! Juliet wills it so. How is't, my soul? Let's talk; it is not day.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. It is, it is! Hie hence, be gone, away! It is the lark that sings so out of tune, Straining harsh discords and unpleasing sharps. Some say the lark makes sweet division; This doth not so, for she divideth us. Some say the lark and loathed toad chang'd eyes; O, now I would they had chang'd voices too, Since arm from arm that voice doth us affray, Hunting thee hence with hunt's-up to the day! O, now be gone! More light and light it grows.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] ROMEO. More light and light- more dark and dark our woes!
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] NURSE. Your lady mother is coming to your chamber. The day is broke; be wary, look about.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Then, window, let day in, and let life out.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] ROMEO. Farewell, farewell! One kiss, and I'll descend. He goeth down.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Art thou gone so, my lord, my love, my friend? I must hear from thee every day in the hour, For in a minute there are many days. O, by this count I shall be much in years Ere I again behold my Romeo!
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] ROMEO. Farewell! I will omit no opportunity That may convey my greetings, love, to thee.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. O, think'st thou we shall ever meet again?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] ROMEO. I doubt it not; and all these woes shall serve For sweet discourses in our time to come.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. O God, I have an ill-divining soul! Methinks I see thee, now thou art below, As one dead in the bottom of a tomb. Either my eyesight fails, or thou look'st pale.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: ROMEO
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] ROMEO. And trust me, love, in my eye so do you. Dry sorrow drinks our blood. Adieu, adieu!
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. O Fortune, Fortune! all men call thee fickle. If thou art fickle, what dost thou with him That is renown'd for faith? Be fickle, Fortune, For then I hope thou wilt not keep him long But send him back.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] LADY. Ho, daughter! are you up?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Who is't that calls? It is my lady mother. Is she not down so late, or up so early? What unaccustom'd cause procures her hither?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] LADY. Why, how now, Juliet?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Madam, I am not well.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] LADY. Evermore weeping for your cousin's death? What, wilt thou wash him from his grave with tears? An if thou couldst, thou couldst not make him live. Therefore have done. Some grief shows much of love; But much of grief shows still some want of wit.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Yet let me weep for such a feeling loss.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] LADY. So shall you feel the loss, but not the friend Which you weep for.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Feeling so the loss, I cannot choose but ever weep the friend.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] LADY. Well, girl, thou weep'st not so much for his death As that the villain lives which slaughter'd him.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. What villain, madam?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] LADY. That same villain Romeo.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Villain and he be many miles asunder.- God pardon him! I do, with all my heart; And yet no man like he doth grieve my heart.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] LADY. That is because the traitor murderer lives.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Ay, madam, from the reach of these my hands. Would none but I might venge my cousin's death!
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] LADY. We will have vengeance for it, fear thou not. Then weep no more. I'll send to one in Mantua, Where that same banish'd runagate doth live, Shall give him such an unaccustom'd dram That he shall soon keep Tybalt company; And then I hope thou wilt be satisfied.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Indeed I never shall be satisfied With Romeo till I behold him- dead- Is my poor heart so for a kinsman vex'd. Madam, if you could find out but a man To bear a poison, I would temper it; That Romeo should, upon receipt thereof, Soon sleep in quiet. O, how my heart abhors To hear him nam'd and cannot come to him, To wreak the love I bore my cousin Tybalt Upon his body that hath slaughter'd him!
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] LADY. Find thou the means, and I'll find such a man. But now I'll tell thee joyful tidings, girl.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. And joy comes well in such a needy time. What are they, I beseech your ladyship?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] LADY. Well, well, thou hast a careful father, child; One who, to put thee from thy heaviness, Hath sorted out a sudden day of joy That thou expects not nor I look'd not for.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Madam, in happy time! What day is that?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] LADY. Marry, my child, early next Thursday morn The gallant, young, and noble gentleman, The County Paris, at Saint Peter's Church, Shall happily make thee there a joyful bride.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Now by Saint Peter's Church, and Peter too, He shall not make me there a joyful bride! I wonder at this haste, that I must wed Ere he that should be husband comes to woo. I pray you tell my lord and father, madam, I will not marry yet; and when I do, I swear It shall be Romeo, whom you know I hate, Rather than Paris. These are news indeed!
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] LADY. Here comes your father. Tell him so yourself, And see how he will take it at your hands.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] CAPULET. When the sun sets the air doth drizzle dew, But for the sunset of my brother's son It rains downright. How now? a conduit, girl? What, still in tears? Evermore show'ring? In one little body Thou counterfeit'st a bark, a sea, a wind: For still thy eyes, which I may call the sea, Do ebb and flow with tears; the bark thy body is Sailing in this salt flood; the winds, thy sighs, Who, raging with thy tears and they with them, Without a sudden calm will overset Thy tempest-tossed body. How now, wife? Have you delivered to her our decree?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] LADY. Ay, sir; but she will none, she gives you thanks. I would the fool were married to her grave!
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] CAPULET. Soft! take me with you, take me with you, wife. How? Will she none? Doth she not give us thanks? Is she not proud? Doth she not count her blest, Unworthy as she is, that we have wrought So worthy a gentleman to be her bridegroom?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Not proud you have, but thankful that you have. Proud can I never be of what I hate, But thankful even for hate that is meant love.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] CAPULET. How now, how now, choplogic? What is this? 'Proud'- and 'I thank you'- and 'I thank you not'- And yet 'not proud'? Mistress minion you, Thank me no thankings, nor proud me no prouds, But fettle your fine joints 'gainst Thursday next To go with Paris to Saint Peter's Church, Or I will drag thee on a hurdle thither. Out, you green-sickness carrion I out, you baggage! You tallow-face!
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] LADY. Fie, fie! what, are you mad?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Good father, I beseech you on my knees, Hear me with patience but to speak a word.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] CAPULET. Hang thee, young baggage! disobedient wretch! I tell thee what- get thee to church a Thursday Or never after look me in the face. Speak not, reply not, do not answer me! My fingers itch. Wife, we scarce thought us blest That God had lent us but this only child; But now I see this one is one too much, And that we have a curse in having her. Out on her, hilding!
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] NURSE. God in heaven bless her! You are to blame, my lord, to rate her so.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] CAPULET. And why, my Lady Wisdom? Hold your tongue,
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: GOOD PRUDENCE
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] GOOD PRUDENCE. Smatter with your gossips, go!
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] NURSE. I speak no treason.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] CAPULET. O, God-i-god-en!
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] NURSE. May not one speak?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] CAPULET. Peace, you mumbling fool! Utter your gravity o'er a gossip's bowl, For here we need it not.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] LADY. You are too hot.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: CAPULET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] CAPULET. God's bread I it makes me mad. Day, night, late, early, At home, abroad, alone, in company, Waking or sleeping, still my care hath been To have her match'd; and having now provided A gentleman of princely parentage, Of fair demesnes, youthful, and nobly train'd, Stuff'd, as they say, with honourable parts, Proportion'd as one's thought would wish a man- And then to have a wretched puling fool, A whining mammet, in her fortune's tender, To answer 'I'll not wed, I cannot love; I am too young, I pray you pardon me'! But, an you will not wed, I'll pardon you. Graze where you will, you shall not house with me. Look to't, think on't; I do not use to jest. Thursday is near; lay hand on heart, advise: An you be mine, I'll give you to my friend; An you be not, hang, beg, starve, die in the streets, For, by my soul, I'll ne'er acknowledge thee, Nor what is mine shall never do thee good. Trust to't. Bethink you. I'll not be forsworn. Exit.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Is there no pity sitting in the clouds That sees into the bottom of my grief? O sweet my mother, cast me not away! Delay this marriage for a month, a week; Or if you do not, make the bridal bed In that dim monument where Tybalt lies.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: LADY
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] LADY. Talk not to me, for I'll not speak a word. Do as thou wilt, for I have done with thee. Exit.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. O God!- O nurse, how shall this be prevented? My husband is on earth, my faith in heaven. How shall that faith return again to earth Unless that husband send it me from heaven By leaving earth? Comfort me, counsel me. Alack, alack, that heaven should practise stratagems Upon so soft a subject as myself! What say'st thou? Hast thou not a word of joy? Some comfort, nurse.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] NURSE. Faith, here it is. Romeo is banish'd; and all the world to nothing That he dares ne'er come back to challenge you; Or if he do, it needs must be by stealth. Then, since the case so stands as now it doth, I think it best you married with the County. O, he's a lovely gentleman! Romeo's a dishclout to him. An eagle, madam, Hath not so green, so quick, so fair an eye As Paris hath. Beshrew my very heart, I think you are happy in this second match, For it excels your first; or if it did not, Your first is dead- or 'twere as good he were As living here and you no use of him.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Speak'st thou this from thy heart?
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] NURSE. And from my soul too; else beshrew them both.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Well, thou hast comforted me marvellous much. Go in; and tell my lady I am gone, Having displeas'd my father, to Laurence' cell, To make confession and to be absolv'd.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: NURSE
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] NURSE. Marry, I will; and this is wisely done. Exit.
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.

speaker: JULIET
text: [Scene: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.] [Keywords: farewell, forced marriage] JULIET. Ancient damnation! O most wicked fiend! Is it more sin to wish me thus forsworn, Or to dispraise my lord with that same tongue Which she hath prais'd him with above compare So many thousand times? Go, counsellor! Thou and my bosom henceforth shall be twain. I'll to the friar to know his remedy. If all else fail, myself have power to die. Exit. SHAKESPEARE IS COPYRIGHT 1990-1993 BY WORLD LIBRARY, INC., AND IS
scene_summary: Romeo and Juliet part, and Juliet resists her father’s marriage plan.
